markdown:
A screaming sulfur is a creature born from gasping death and suffocating suffering. When an earthquake or other geological phenomena releases toxic gases, these gases can build up beneath lakes and other large bodies of water. Eventually, they escape the water, expanding into massive clouds that cover the immediate area. Its silent, unexpected arrival can take thousands of lives in an instant. Confused, in pain, and desperate for a way out, the dying souls become trapped in the noxious cloud and amass into this horrifying creature. While not truly [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160), a screaming sulfur shares many similarities with undead creatures, including the malice of vengeful spirits and the hatred of all living creatures who breathe freely and painlessly. A screaming sulfur resembles a cloud made of countless, featureless faces, mouths agape and releasing ghastly wails. **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Screaming Sulfur](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1659) **Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 84 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +22 **Str** -5 **Dex** +7 **Con** +0 **Int** +3 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +3 **Site Bound** A screaming sulfur becomes bound to the location where the people whose souls make up its form died. It can stray up to 1,000 feet from this site of death, or up to 100 feet from the edges of any waterway associated with its death, whichever distance is shorter. --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +20 **Will** +20 **HP** 125 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation) **Immunities** [death](/Domains.aspx?ID=7) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Agonizing Cries** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 30 feet. The screaming sulfur constantly shrieks with the pained wails of countless spirits. A living creature that enters or starts its turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 26 Will save or become [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) as long as it remains in the aura (frightened 2 on a critical failure). A creature that succeeds is temporarily immune for 1 hour.
**Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Blessing the site where the screaming sulfur was formed and burying whatever dead remain allows the spirits within the screaming sulfur to move on to the afterlife, rendering it inert. --- **Speed** fly 40 feet **Melee** ghostly hand +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+15 negative damage **Choke** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The screaming sulfur bombards all creatures within its Agonizing Cries aura with visions of death and a feeling of suffocation. Each creature takes 4d6 mental damage (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save).
Screams of the Sodden Lands The countless lakes and waterways of both the Sodden Lands and Terwa Lands are rife with trapped gases, providing the perfect conditions for the creation of screaming sulfurs. These occurrences are common enough in the region that Lirgeni and Yamasan populations refer to the waterways near their homes as “Assassin Lakes,” due to the silent method through which these gases kill others. Whether or not a given waterway will be a cause of death is unclear, but many people in the Sodden Lands avoid taking chances, building their homes on stilts to avoid the unexpected release of deadly fog.
name: Screaming Sulfur

Ssumzilis are lanky, murderous fey who delight in the thrill of the hunt. They are particularly skilled/adept at stalking their prey in the rain and most commonly reside in rainforests and coastal regions, where frequent rain provides ample opportunity to hunt. Camouflaged by their cloud-gray skin, ssumzilis often compete in tests of stealth when hunting in groups, taking particular delight in approaching their quarry as closely as possible without being observed. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Ssumzili](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1660) **Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 85 **Perception** +25; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +19, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +25, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +21 **Str** +5 **Dex** +7 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +3 **Deluge Vision** A ssumzili ignores sensory difficulties caused by rain.
**Precipitation Camouflage** A ssumzili can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) and [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63) in rainfall, even if it doesn't have cover. --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +25 **Will** +23 **HP** 201 **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 10 **Standing Water** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) 10 feet. When it rains, the ground near a ssumzili becomes slick. When raining, all terrain within 10 feet of a ssumzili is slippery, with the effects of [_grease_](/Spells.aspx?ID=140) (DC 29). Ssumzilis are immune to this effect. --- **Speed** 40 feet; puddle stride **Melee** claw +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 3d8+7 slashing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 29 - **1st** [Negate Aroma](/Spells.aspx?ID=206) - **2nd** [Pass Without Trace](/Spells.aspx?ID=215), [Quench](/Spells.aspx?ID=709) (at will), [Water Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=371) - **3rd** [Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=94) - **4th** [Hydraulic Torrent](/Spells.aspx?ID=155) - **5th** [Transmute Rock and Mud](/Spells.aspx?ID=724) (rock to mud only) - **Constant (2nd)** [Endure Elements](/Spells.aspx?ID=99) (cold) **Puddle Stride** A ssumzili ignores difficult terrain caused by rain, such as puddles or wet rocks. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** A ssumzili deals an additional 2d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures. **Soaking Strike** The ssumzili makes a melee Strike. If the Strike damages the target, the target must also attempt a DC 32 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature becomes soaked, causing them to treat the [environmental temperature](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) as one step colder (mild to severe, severe to extreme, and so on) until they dry off.
**Failure** As success, and the creature is [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As failure but the fatigue lasts until the target gets a full night's rest.
Hunting Patterns of Ssumzilis Being ambush hunters, ssumzilis tend to avoid hunting without the cover of rainfall whenever possible. A ssumzili encountered hunting on a sunny day is doing so out of desperation and is likely to be unpredictable and erratic as a result.
name: Ssumzili

Ba'aupa mdoudus are solitary, bone-eating creatures that prefer to feed on remains found on battlefields or within necropolises—the more ancient, the better. When unable to locate buried remains, ba'aupa mdoudus are drawn reluctantly to the surface in search of bones from the living; living bones can sustain a ba'aupa mdoudu, but the flavor (particularly the temperature of warm bones fresh from the flesh) unsettles them a bit. Ba'aupa mdoudus produce a slimy acid that liquefies minerals, allowing them to burrow though even solid stone and strip natural deposits of calcium from nearby rocks. In doing so, they leave smooth tunnels in their wake. When provoked, ba'aupa mdoudus secrete this acid through numerous pores along their length as a defensive response and accelerate this secretion under extreme circumstances. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Ba'aupa Mdoudu](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1661) **Source** [Pathfinder #172: Secrets of the Temple City](/Sources.aspx?ID=102) pg. 80 **Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), skeleton sense 60 feet, tremorsense (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +25 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** -4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** -2 **Skeleton Sense** A ba'aupa mdoudu can sense the presence of bones and skeletons within 60 feet; this is a precise sense when detecting dead bones but an imprecise one when detecting living bones. --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +21 **Will** +23 **HP** 245 **Immunities** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3) **Resistances** bludgeoning 15 **Reactive** A ba'aupa mdoudu gains an additional reaction at the start of its turn but can use this reaction only to Slime Squirt.
**Slime Squirt** **Trigger** A creature infiicts slashing or piercing damage on the ba'aupa mdoudu with a melee Strike; **Efiect** The attacking creature takes 2d10 acid damage. --- **Speed** 30 feet, burrow 20 feet **Melee** jaws +27 ([deadly d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=174)), **Damage** 3d8+14 piercing plus 1d10 [persistent acid](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Ranged** spit +23 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 6d6 acid **Caustic Acid** Whenever a creature takes acid damage from any of a ba'aupa mdoudu's abilities or Strikes, it must succeed at a DC 30 Fortitude save or become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (drained 2 on a critical failure). On each subsequent failure, the drained condition value increases by 1 (or by 2 on a critical failure), to a maximum of drained 4. **Caustic Rain** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The ba'aupa mdoudu spews copious amounts of acid into the air, creating a cascading rain to fall in a 10- foot radius around itself. All creatures in the area take 14d6 acid damage (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The ba'aupa mdoudu can't use Caustic Rain again for 1d4 rounds. **Stone Tunneler** A ba'aupa mdoudu can leave a tunnel when it burrows through earth or stone, and it usually does.
Inorganic Treasure The caustic acid of the ba'aupa mdoudu is extremely corrosive to organic material and stone but has minimal effect on metal and gems. Tombs raided by the ba'aupa mdoudu are thus typically devoid of any remains or organic materials; however, armor, weapons, and other inorganic artifacts are normally found intact.
name: Ba'aupa Mdoudu

markdown:
When great heroes or other powerful individuals encounter misfortune and die at sea or to other water-related accidents, the anguished soul sometimes clings to its corpse, creating a drenchdead. Drenchdead prefer to lurk in water and attack from ambush, giving victims the same deadly surprise that led to their own deaths. A drenchdead can manipulate water to drown a victim directly but prefers to draw out this suffering. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Drenchdead](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1662) **Source** [Pathfinder #172: Secrets of the Temple City](/Sources.aspx?ID=102) pg. 81 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [wavesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=41) (precise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +21, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +23, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +19 **Str** +7 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +21 **Will** +19 **HP** 230 **Immunities** [death](/Domains.aspx?ID=7) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 10 --- **Speed** 20 feet, swim 35 feet **Melee** claw +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 3d6+13 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** jaws +26, **Damage** 3d10+13 piercing **Aquatic Camouflage** When fully submerged in water, the drenchdead can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) from other creatures even if it doesn't have cover against them and isn't [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) from them. **Drag Down** **Requirements** The drenchdead is swimming in water at least 10 feet deep; **Effect** The drenchdead attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) an adjacent creature (on nearby land or in the water). On a success against a creature on land, in addition to the normal effects of Grapple, the drenchdead pulls the creature into the water into a space adjacent to itself. If the creature is already in the water, on a success, in addition the normal effects of Grapple, the drenchdead drags the creature 10 feet deeper, moving 10 feet with the creature. Moving a creature into water or deeper into water using Drag Down is [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) for the target creature, but not for the drenchdead. **Drowning Touch** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) The drenchdead touches a creature and causes water from its own body to flow into the creature's lungs. If the creature can't breathe water, it must attempt a DC 32 Fortitude save.
**Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Drowning Touch for 24 hours.
**Failure** The creature becomes [sickened 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
**Critical Failure** The creature chokes on the water and runs out of air. It falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) and starts [drowning](/Rules.aspx?ID=468). If the creature is above water, it recovers from drowning as soon as it succeeds at a saving throw against suffocation. **Surface Skimmer** A drenchdead submerged in water has cover from attacks made by creatures out of the water.
Drenchdead on Golarion Drenchdead are common along coasts but are particularly prevalent in the Shackles and near the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. Great pirates might meet their death at [Besmara's](/Deities.aspx?ID=57) hands just as they were returning home with a massive haul of treasure, or viking raiders could die on their journey across the ocean toward Vallenhall. These proud heroes might rise as [draugr](/Monsters.aspx?ID=642) or other [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160), but the most notable heroes of all become drenchdead.
name: Drenchdead

Graveknight captains once proudly led squads of troops in battle. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Graveknight Captain](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1663) **Source** [Pathfinder #172: Secrets of the Temple City](/Sources.aspx?ID=102) pg. 82 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +15, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +12, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +12 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 **Items** _+1 [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)_, graveknight's shield, _[shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303) [warhammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=47)_ --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +14 **Will** +13 **HP** 90 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Graveknight's Shield** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The graveknight's curse extends to their shield, or the graveknight's armor uses a portion of itself to produce a shield. The graveknight has a shield that uses the statistics of a [_sturdy shield_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=327) of a level no higher than the graveknight's level – 1. The shield is quasi-independent of the graveknight and automatically protects the graveknight from harm. When the shield is raised, it automatically uses [Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32) to reduce the damage of the first attack against the graveknight each round without the graveknight needing to spend their reaction to do so. The shield automatically rejuvenates with the rest of the graveknight and must be destroyed in the same manner as the graveknight's armor.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _warhammer_ +16 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)), **Damage** 2d8+7 bludgeoning plus 1d6 electricity **Melee** fist +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56)), **Damage** 2d4+7 bludgeoning plus 1d6 electricity **Devastating Blast** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The graveknight unleashes a 30-foot cone of electricity. Creatures in the area take 4d12 electricity damage(DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The graveknight can use this ability once every 1d4 rounds. **Graveknight's Curse** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) This curse affects anyone who wears a graveknight's armor for at least 1 hour. **Saving Throw** DC 24 Will save; **Onset** 1 hour; **Stage 1** [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) and cannot remove the armor (1 day); **Stage 2** doomed 2, hampered 10, and cannot remove the armor (1 day); **Stage 3** dies and transforms into the armor's graveknight. **Weapon Master** The graveknight has access to the [critical specialization effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235) of any weapons it wields.
name: Graveknight Captain

Once the servant of a deity, the graveknight champion returned as an undead after a life cut short in service to their god. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Graveknight Champion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1664) **Source** [Pathfinder #172: Secrets of the Temple City](/Sources.aspx?ID=102) pg. 83 **Perception** +27; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +31, [Deity Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +29, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +27 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Items** _[greater flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) [composite shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75)_ (20 arrows), _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)_, _[greater flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) [greatpick](/Weapons.aspx?ID=23)_ --- **AC** 38 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +26 **Will** +25 **HP** 275 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Clutching Armor** ([ <%TRAITS%11%%> arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11) <%END>, [ <%TRAITS%157%%> transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157) <%END>) **Trigger** A creature attempts to move away from the graveknight; **Effect** The graveknight's armor animates and attempts to Grab the triggering creature. It makes an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) using the graveknight's Athletics modifier – 2. The armor can continue to Grapple the creature normally. Since the armor is grappling the creature, the graveknight doesn't need a free hand to do so. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _greatpick_ +31 ([fatal d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=178), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d10+16 slashing plus 1d6 fire **Melee** fist +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)), **Damage** 3d6+16 bludgeoning plus 1d6 fire **Ranged** _composite shortbow_ +28 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 3d6+10 piercing plus 1d6 fire **Innate Divine Spells** DC 33, attack +25 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **4th** [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=119) - **5th** [Cone of Cold](/Spells.aspx?ID=47), [Flame Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=120) - **6th** [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=119), [Scorching Ray](/Spells.aspx?ID=992) - **7th** [Eclipse Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=96), [Finger of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=116) **Channel Magic** The graveknight redirects magical energies through its armor, allowing it to deliver magic through an attack. The graveknight Casts a Spell that takes 1 or 2 actions to cast and requires a spell attack roll. The effects of the spell don't occur immediately but are imbued into an attack instead. The graveknight then makes a melee Strike with a weapon or [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack. The spell is coupled with the attack, using the attack roll result to determine the effects of both the Strike and the spell. This counts as two attacks for the graveknight's multiple attack penalty but doesn't apply the penalty until after it's completed Channeling Magic. The graveknight can't use Channel Magic again for 1d4 rounds. **Devastating Blast** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) The graveknight unleashes a 30-foot cone of fire. Creatures in the area take 8d12 fire damage(DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The graveknight can use this ability once every 1d4 rounds. **Graveknight's Curse** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) This curse affects anyone who wears a graveknight's armor for at least 1 hour. **Saving Throw** DC 24 Will save; **Onset** 1 hour; **Stage 1** [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) and cannot remove the armor (1 day); **Stage 2** doomed 2, hampered 10, and cannot remove the armor (1 day); **Stage 3** dies and transforms into the armor's graveknight. **Weapon Master** The graveknight has access to the [critical specialization effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235) of any weapons it wields.
name: Graveknight Champion

Beautiful and dangerous, kilia mwibos appear as lush, green trees with brightly colored and appealing fleshy seedpods that remain attached year round. Over time, these seedpods become hollow and create an eerie, forlorn wail that generates feelings of sadness and despair. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Kilia Mwibo](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1665) **Source** [Pathfinder #172: Secrets of the Temple City](/Sources.aspx?ID=102) pg. 84 **Perception** +26; emotion sense 60 feet **Languages** [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +30, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +28 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** -2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +0 **Emotion Sense** A kilia mwibo can sense nearby negative emotions. This is a precise sense against creatures who are under powerful [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effects like [rage](/Actions.aspx?ID=3), those who are [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), or, at the GM's discretion, those who are in the grips of hatred, sadness, or other overwhelming negative emotions. Against all other creatures that possess emotions, this is an imprecise sense. A creature that has no emotions is effectively invisible to a kilia mwibo. A creature that knows about emotion sense can take precautions to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) and [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63) from a kilia mwibo, in this case by suppressing feelings of negativity. You can flnd more details on hiding from special senses [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=414). --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +29 **Will** +26 **HP** 345 **Immunities** critical hits, precision, vision-based effects **Resistances** [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147) 15 **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 15, slashing 15 **Despairing Presence** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 60 feet. A creature that enters the emanation or starts its turn within it tumbles down a deep well of dread and must attempt a DC 33 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target becomes [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
**Failure** The target becomes frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The target becomes frightened 3 and [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) until it's no longer frightened.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** Frond only. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** frond +30 ([deadly d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 3d10+15 slashing plus 1d6 bleed and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** root +28 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 3d8+15 bludgeoning **Ranged** thorn +28 ([deadly d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d6+15 piercing **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 2d12+15, DC 36 **Consume Emotion** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The kilia mwibo feeds on the negative emotions of a creature within 60 feet; this must be a creature that it can precisely sense with its emotion sense. The target must succeed at a DC 36 Will save or increase its [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) condition value by 1 (by 2 on a critical failure), to a maximum of drained 4.
Fear Feeders Instead of drawing sustenance from light and soil, kilia mwibos gorge themselves on the negative emotions of creatures, eventually draining them of their life force and discarding their carcasses. When creatures recognize the true nature of the kilia mwibos and attempt to flee, the trees send out buried roots to trip and slow them down before grabbing them with their thorny fronds.
name: Kilia Mwibo

markdown:
Warlords and heroes alike often fell their enemies and leave the corpses behind, exposed to the elements. [Undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) tend to rise in the dark of night and shun the cleansing rays of the sun, but this isn't the rule for all undead. The corpse of a particularly evil individual left exposed to the constant beating of the sun might become a sunburst corpse. These undead radiate light and wield sunlight as a weapon. Sunburst corpses initially seek revenge for their deaths but thereafter wander with undirected rage. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Sunburst Corpse](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1666) **Source** [Pathfinder #172: Secrets of the Temple City](/Sources.aspx?ID=102) pg. 85 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +23, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +29, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +25 **Str** +5 **Dex** +1 **Con** +8 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +7 **Light Camouflage** The sunburst corpse is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) in areas of natural bright light, such as due to sunlight or [torches](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60), even to creatures that can see clearly in bright light. Magical bright light and light from the sunburst corpse's blinding aura don't conceal the sunburst corpse. --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +21 **Will** +25 **HP** 255 **Immunities** [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1), [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 10, darkness vulnerability **Blinding Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) 60 feet. The sunburst corpse sheds bright light. Any creature that starts its turn in the emanation must succeed at a DC 31 Fortitude save. If it fails, it's [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 minute, and if it critically fails, it's blinded permanently. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune to this effect for 24 hours.
**Darkness Vulnerability** A sunburst corpse is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) while in areas of magical [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39). If the level of the magical darkness effect is at least half of the sunburst corpse's level (a 7th-level effect against most sunburst corpses), the sunburst corpse is also [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) while in the area. --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** claw +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 3d6+13 slashing plus 2d8 fire and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) and residual light **Ranged** sunfire +25 ([range increment 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 7d6 fire **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 2d6+6 bludgeoning plus 2d8 fire and residual light, DC 34 **Nova Burst** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The sunburst corpse intensifies its light and unleashes a burning blast of energy. It deals 12d8 fire damage to all creatures in a 20-foot emanation (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). A creature that fails its save becomes [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the end of its next turn. The sunburst corpse can't use Nova Burst again for 1d4 rounds, and its blinding aura becomes inactive during this time. **Residual Light** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) When a sunburst corpse Strikes or Grapples a creature, some of the corpse's light clings to the creature for a moment. The creature must attempt a DC 34 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the end of its next turn. On a critical failure, the creature is dazzled for 1 minute. A dazzled creature can use an Interact action to wick off the light and remove the dazzled condition.
Deathless Light A sunburst corpse's dead flesh is a [spell catalyst](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1452).
name: Sunburst Corpse

<title level="1" pfs="">[Formian Worker](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1670)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

By far the most common formian on Akiton, workers are focused and industrious, naturally skilled at manual labor and coordinating with peers.

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Formian_Worker.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Formian Worker](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1670)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="LN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Formian" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=419" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #173: Doorway to the Red Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=103) pg. 82

**Perception** +6; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet

**Languages**
[Akitonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=94), [Formian](/Languages.aspx?ID=95); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 30 feet

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +7, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +6

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +1

**Con** +3

**Int** +0

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Hive Mind** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) Formians operate from a shared hive intelligence that allows them to communicate nearly instantaneously. While within telepathic range of at least one other formian from the same hive mind, a formian worker gains a +2 circumstance bonus to initiative checks, Perception checks, and saving throws against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects. If one formian is aware of a combatant, all members of the hive mind within range are aware of it.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 16 

**Fort** +8 

**Ref** +4 

**Will** +6 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 20

</row>

**Resistances**
[sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147) 2

**Able Assistant** When using the [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=75) action to assist another formian from the same hive mind within range of its hive link, if the formian worker rolls a success, they get a critical success instead, and if they roll a critical failure, they get a failure instead.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, burrow 5 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +7,
**Damage** 1d4+4 slashing

**Heavy Lifter**   Formian workers excel at heavy labor. A formian worker can carry 5 additional bulk beyond the normal limits for their Strength before becoming encumbered or overburdened.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-293" />
name: Formian Worker

markdown:
Grim, twisted formians forced into existence through the machinations of the King of Biting Ants, formian mageslayers are created for the express purpose of neutralizing and destroying enemy spellcasters. Wired to never sleep, mageslayers are tortured hunters and guardians whose only relief is found in tearing apart the magic and flesh of spellcasters who oppose the King of Biting Ants. Formian mageslayers have wings resembling those of dragonflies and longer, sleeker bodies than most formians. [Formian Mageslayer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1671) **Source** [Pathfinder #173: Doorway to the Red Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=103) pg. 83 **Perception** +31; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet, [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) 60 feet **Languages** [Akitonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=94), [Formian](/Languages.aspx?ID=95) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +28, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +32, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +30, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +32, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +30, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +28, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +28 **Str** +5 **Dex** +8 **Con** +5 **Int** +9 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +5 **Disrupting Aura** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 30 feet. Formian mageslayers are surrounded by an aura of disruptive energy that disperses magical energy and makes spellcasting difficult for their enemies. Whenever an enemy creature within the aura attempts to Cast a Spell or Activate a magic item, they must roll a DC 5 flat check; on a failed check, the spell or activation is lost and the action is wasted. Free action activations disrupted by this aura can't be attempted again until the start of the activating creature's next turn.
**Hive Mind** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) Formians operate from a shared hive intelligence that allows them to communicate nearly instantaneously. While within telepathic range of at least one other formian from the same hive mind, a formian worker gains a +2 circumstance bonus to initiative checks, Perception checks, and saving throws against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects. If one formian is aware of a combatant, all members of the hive mind within range are aware of it. --- **AC** 38 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +28 **Will** +30 **HP** 240 **Immunities** [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147) 15, spell resistance **Spell Resistance** A formian mageslayer takes 15 fewer damage from any arcane or primal spell that causes damage, and 10 less damage from any divine or occult spell that causes damage.
**Shred Spell** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Trigger** A creature within the formian mageslayer's disrupting aura that the formian mageslayer can see succeeds at a DC 5 flat check to Cast the Spell in the aura; **Effect** The mageslayer attempts to unravel the spell's magic and attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the spell. They use their [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) modifier as their counteract modifier with a counteract level of 8. If the formian mageslayer succeeds, they counter the spell and use the dispersed energy to gain a number of temporary Hit Points equal to twice the spell's level for 1 minute. If the formian mageslayer critically fails this counteract check, their disrupting aura deactivates for 1 minute. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** claw +32 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 3d10+13 slashing plus 3d6 [persistent mental damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and stupefying touch **Ranged** acid spit +32 ([range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d6+8 acid plus 3d6 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Stupefying Touch** ([Abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) A creature damaged by a mageslayer's claw Strike must succeed at a DC 37 Will save. The creature becomes [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round on a failure. On a critical failure, the creature is stupefied 2 for 1 hour.
name: Formian Mageslayer

markdown:
The formian queen is the center of the formian hive. The queen identifies and attends to the hive's needs, laying egg clusters that vary from a single formian born to a particular task to hundreds of workers at a time. All formians are born with an instinctive deference for the queen that borders on slavish devotion, though this urge can be corrupted, as with the King of Biting Ants' mutant mageslayers. While within telepathic range of at least one other formian from the same hive mind, a formian worker gains a +2 circumstance bonus to initiative checks, Perception checks, and saving throws against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects. If one formian is aware of a combatant, all members of the hive mind within range are aware of it. --- **AC** 40 **Fort** +34 **Ref** +26 **Will** +31 **HP** 255 **Resistances** all physical 15, [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147) 15 --- **Speed** 10 feet **Melee** claw +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+12 slashing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 38, attack +30 - **7th** [Magic Missile](/Spells.aspx?ID=180) (at will) - **8th** [Disappearance](/Spells.aspx?ID=73), [Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=161), [Repulsion](/Spells.aspx?ID=254), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338) - **9th** [Cone of Cold](/Spells.aspx?ID=47), [Feeblemind](/Spells.aspx?ID=112) **Rituals** - **6th** [Commune with Nature](/Rituals.aspx?ID=7) **Hive Frenzy** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The formian queen stirs all formians within range of her telepathy into a frenzy. Each affected formian gains the [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) condition for 1 minute, and can use the extra action each round only to Strike or Stride. **Possess Formian** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Possession](/Traits.aspx?ID=129), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) A formian queen transfers her consciousness to the body of any formian within range of her telepathy to whom she has a line of effect. As long as the queen's consciousness is outside her body, her body is [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) (though she is aware if any damage is dealt to it). An unwilling target can attempt a DC 38 Will saving throw to resist being possessed, but the queen's offspring are part of her hive mind and thus are almost always willing. The queen receives all sensory input from the target creature; she can speak through it, and she can use her own modifier for Intelligence-, Wisdom-, and Charisma-based skill checks. If the target creature is slain or moves to a different plane from the queen's body, the queen returns to her own body immediately and is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36); otherwise, she can return to her own body as a free action, ending the possession effect. **Telepathic Feedback** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The formian queen unleashes a blast of debilitating telepathic feedback, targeting as many creatures as she wishes within range of her telepathy and to whom she has a line of effect. Targeted creatures must attempt a DC 38 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Telepathic Feedback for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature takes 3d6 mental damage, 1d6 [persistent mental damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and is sickened 1.
**Failure** The creature takes 6d6 mental damage, 1d6 persistent mental damage, and is [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 12d6 mental damage, 1d6 persistent mental damage, and is [stunned 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36). Upon recovering from the stunned condition, the creature becomes sickened 2.
name: Formian Queen

markdown:
Akiton's gluttonworms share many features of Golarion's [cave worms](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=21), yet these immense predators are their own distinct species. Gluttonworms “swim” through the vast deserts of Akiton, moving almost like whales through the ocean as they forage for their next meals. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Gluttonworm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1673) **Source** [Pathfinder #173: Doorway to the Red Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=103) pg. 85 **Perception** +32; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) (imprecise) 100 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +37 **Str** +10 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** -4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** -1 --- **AC** 41 **Fort** +35 **Ref** +29 **Will** +32 **HP** 445 **Dune Dispersal** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206)) 15 feet. Ground in the aura is greater difficult terrain.
**Sand Armor** ([necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) A gluttonworm in contact with a significant body of sand, such as a desert or beach, gains [fast healing 10](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15). If the gluttonworm is subject to [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), the flowing blood quickly congeals with the sand covering its body, instantly ending the bleed duration and giving the gluttonworm a +2 circumstance bonus to AC until the end of its next turn.
**Inexorable** The gluttonworm recovers from the [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35), and [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) conditions at the end of its turn. It's also immune to penalties to its Speeds and the [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) condition, and it ignores difficult terrain and greater difficult terrain. --- **Speed** 30 feet, burrow 40 feet (in sand or loose soil only) **Melee** jaws +37 ([deadly 3d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d12+18 piercing and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** body +33, **Damage** 4d8+18 bludgeoning **Fast Swallow** **Trigger** The gluttonworm Grabs a creature, or a creature fails a Reflex save against a Gulping Charge; **Effect** The gluttonworm uses Swallow Whole. **Gulping Charge** The gluttonworm Strides up to double its Speed and can move through the spaces of Large or smaller creatures, gulping each creature whose space the worm enters and attempting a body Strike against each of them. The gluttonworm can use this Strike on each creature only once in a single use of Gulping Charge. Creatures can attempt a DC 41 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against this damage. **Spew Sandstorm** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The gluttonworm exhales swirling sand in a 60-foot cone. Creatures in the area must attempt a DC 41 Reflex save. It can't Spew Sandstorm again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 4d6 slashing damage and 4d6 bludgeoning damage from the scouring sand.
**Failure** The creature takes 8d6 slashing damage and 8d6 bludgeoning damage from the sand, and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the end of its next turn.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 12d6 slashing damage and 12d6 bludgeoning damage and is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 hour. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Huge, 3d10+17 bludgeoning plus 2d6 slashing, Rupture 36
Forever Ravenous A gluttonworm's metabolism is slow; they can go for years or even decades without a filling meal. Most gluttonworms exist in a constant state of ravenous pain as a result, and behave in an incredibly aggressive manner when they encounter anything edible. Most native creatures know to stay well away from the telltale rumbling and shifting dunes that portend a gluttonworm's approach.
Thasteron This unusual mineral can be found throughout Akiton. While not suitable for crafting items, the shimmering greenish ore that contains thasteron can be refined into a volatile liquid fuel—either through technological methods or, more efficiently, through the use of khefak, arthropods who feed on the ore and secrete refined thasteron. Thasteron can be used to fuel a wide range of devices, ranging from weapons to appliances to vehicles. The fuel is slightly radioactive—not enough to cause immediate harm, but prolonged exposure can cause long-term health concerns for most living creatures. A significant part of Akiton's economy is based on the mining, refining, and trade of thasteron, but off-planet the liquid is relatively worthless without proper technology to take advantage of it. Prices for raw thasteron aren't included in this adventure as a result. A character foolish enough to ingest thasteron must make a DC 30 Fortitude save or become [sickened 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (and also drained 1 on a critical failure). Thasteron can be used to create alchemical bombs as well—these bombs are identical to [alchemist's fire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=75).
name: Gluttonworm

Thasteron khefaks are alchemically enhanced versions of standard khefaks, capable of converting the minerals they eat into thasteron. While thasteron remains highly valuable as fuel and for specialized weapons, finding an experienced thasteron khefak breeder is hard because the creatures become radioactive over time. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Thasteron Khefak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1677) **Source** [Pathfinder #173: Doorway to the Red Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=103) pg. 87 **Perception** +9; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +9, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +9 **Str** +3 **Dex** +2 **Con** +3 **Int** -5 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -3 **Consume Objects** A khefak can gain nourishment by eating objects with hardness 10 or lower. When attacking an object with its mandibles, it ignores the first 10 points of that object's Hardness.
**Radiation Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), radiation) 10 feet. Thasteron khefaks convert radioactive minerals they eat into thasteron, a combustive fuel. The more radioactive elements of the material concentrates in their chitin and taints the air around them. A creature who begins their turn within this aura must succeed at a DC 20 Fortitude saving throw or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (sickened 2 on a critical failure). --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +9 **Will** +7 **HP** 42 **Immunities** radiation **Resistances** 5 [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) --- **Speed** 20 feet, burrow 5 feet, climb 20 feet **Melee** mandibles +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 1d8+5 piercing
name: Thasteron Khefak

In rough Akitonian cities like Seldo, shobhad bruisers and fist-fighters act as protection for businesses and enforcers for crime bosses. Shobhad enforcers are more rare and far more dangerous. In the wilds of Akiton, shobhad enforcers protect wealthy caravans using combat techniques honed and proven over multiple generations to devastate any creature that might mistake the caravan as an attractive target for a raid. “Enforcer” is a loose translation of a word in the Shobhad language that is only granted to a warrior who has proven themselves in combat against one of the deadly threats of the Akitonian desert, such as [gluttonworms](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1673) or [ikeshti riveners](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1675). Every enforcer is a battle-tested veteran with the scars to prove their prowess. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Shobhad Enforcer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1678) **Source** [Pathfinder #173: Doorway to the Red Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=103) pg. 88 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Akitonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=94), [Shobhad](/Languages.aspx?ID=97) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +31, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +30, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +30, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +29 **Str** +7 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 **Items** _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=24)_, _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [handwraps of mighty blows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=441)_, _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [chainmail armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=9)_ --- **AC** 38 **Fort** +31 **Ref** +26 **Will** +27 **HP** 320 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**[Ferocity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=16)** --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** _greatsword_ +31 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d10+15 slashing plus 3d6 additional precision damage **Melee** _fist_ +31 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)), **Damage** 2d4+15 bludgeoning plus 3d6 additional precision damage **Brutal Blows** **Requirements** The shobhad enforcer has at least two free hands; **Effect** The shobhad enforcer makes a fist Strike against an adjacent creature. If the Strike hits, the enforcer automatically [Grapples](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) its target and makes a second fist Strike with the following success conditions. The second Strike applies the enforcer's multiple attack penalty normally.
**Critical Success** The target is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36), and [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round.
**Success** The target is stupefied 1 for 1 round.
name: Shobhad Enforcer

Enemies rarely see a shobhad sniper before the deadly blast of their powerful rifles snatches away the offender's life. Selected from among the most talented enforcers after proving themselves in combat, shobhad snipers learn how to cross the desert sands undetected, combine their powerful ranged weapons with close-combat techniques, and survive Akiton's freezing nights for weeks on end while scouting potential settlements or enemy strongholds. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Shobhad Sniper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1679) **Source** [Pathfinder #173: Doorway to the Red Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=103) pg. 89 **Perception** +30; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Akitonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=94), [Shobhad](/Languages.aspx?ID=97) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +31, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +30, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +34, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +30 **Str** +6 **Dex** +9 **Con** +5 **Int** +2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +2 **Items** _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [shobhad longrifle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=251)_, _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [handwraps of mighty blows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=441)_, _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)_ --- **AC** 40 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +32 **Will** +26 **HP** 320 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**[Ferocity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=16)** --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** _fist_ +31 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)), **Damage** 2d10+14 bludgeoning **Ranged** _longrifle_ +34 ([Backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=172), [Concussive](/Traits.aspx?ID=401), [fatal d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=178), [Kickback](/Traits.aspx?ID=409), [Volley](/Traits.aspx?ID=201), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d8+9 bludgeoning; this includes the additional damage from kickback **Sniping Shot** The shobhad sniper makes a Strike with their longrifle, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to this Strike's attack roll and ignoring the target's [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4). **Sniper's Devastation** The shobhad sniper makes a fist Strike against a Medium or smaller creature. If this attack is successful, the shobhad sniper deals no damage, but they immediately [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) and throw their target up to 30 feet in a direction of their choice and then make a ranged Strike with their longrifle against the target, after which the target falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) in the designated location. As normal, the creature doesn't take any additional falling damage from the throw.
name: Shobhad Sniper

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Onyiji](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1680) **Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 9 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +16, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +14, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +16 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +2 **Int** +4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12)_ --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +14 **Will** +16 **HP** 140 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Orc Superstition** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Trigger** Onyiji is about to make a saving throw against a spell or magical effect; **Effect** Onyiji gains a +1 circumstance bonus to the triggering save. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _staff_ +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [two-handed d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=198)), **Damage** 2d6+8 bludgeoning **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 26, attack +18 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236), [Ray of Frost](/Spells.aspx?ID=245), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **2nd** [Shocking Grasp](/Spells.aspx?ID=283), [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) **Spellstrike** **Frequency** twice per day; **Effect** Onyiji casts a spell and couples it with a staff Strike. She casts [_produce flame_](/Spells.aspx?ID=236), [_ray of frost_](/Spells.aspx?ID=245), or [_shocking grasp_](/Spells.aspx?ID=283), but the effects don't occur immediately. She makes a melee Strike with her staff and uses the attack roll result as the effect of both the Strike and the spell. This counts as two attacks towards her multiple attack penalty, but the penalties don't apply until after the Spellstrike. **Twisting Tree** When Onyiji wields a [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12) in one hand, it gains the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170) trait and its damage die size increases to d6 (these adjustments are reflected in the Strike above). When she wields a staff in two hands, it gains the [parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=190), [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), and [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196) traits. She can Interact to Release or change her grip on her staff as a free action when she Strikes with the staff (including Strikes made in a Spellstrike).
name: Onyiji

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Bog Mummy Amalgamation](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1681) **Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 20 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +16 **Str** +6 **Dex** +1 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +15 **Will** +20 **HP** 160 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10 **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 10 **Breath of Silt** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 30 feet. A creature that begins its turn within the area feels as if its lungs are filling with silt and must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude save or be unable to speak or breathe. The creature can still hold its breath and can attempt a new saving throw at the end of its turn. A creature that succeeds is temporarily immune to breath of silt for 24 hours.
**Rise Up** **Trigger** A creature walks on top of the bog mummy amalgamation lying buried in the silt below; **Requirements** Initiative has not yet been rolled; **Effect** The bog mummy amalgamation notices the creature automatically and Burrows before rolling initiative. --- **Speed** 20 feet; burrow 15 feet **Melee** fist +20 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6+5 plus bog rot **Bog Rot** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) This affliction can't be reduced below stage 1, nor can the damage from it be healed, until it's successfully treated with [_remove curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=250) or a similar effect; the affliction can then be removed as normal for a disease. A creature killed by bog rot melts into a noxious sludge and can't be resurrected except by a 7th-level [_resurrect_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=20) ritual or similar magic; **Saving Throw** DC 21 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect (1 minute); **Stage 2** 3d6 negative damage and [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) (1 day). **Fist Flurry** The amalgamation makes two fist Strikes against two different targets within its reach. Its multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after both attacks.
name: Bog Mummy Amalgamation

**[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 37 **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 37 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Ixamè](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1682) **Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 22 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), mist vision, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Auran](/Languages.aspx?ID=15), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +20, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +22, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +16 **Str** +7 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 **Mist Vision** Fog and mist don't impair a cloud dragon's vision; Ixamè ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from fog and mist. --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +18 **Will** +20 **HP** 175 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 90 feet, DC 26
**Deflecting Cloud** **Trigger** Ixamè is the target of a ranged attack; **Requirements** Ixamè is aware of the attack and has a free wing; **Effect** Ixamè rapidly flexes one of her wings and creates a billowing cloud of mist. She is treated as if she were [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) for the purposes of resolving the triggering attack, so normally the attacker must succeed at a DC 11 flat check to target her. Ixamè also gains a +4 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 140 feet; cloud walk **Melee** jaws +23 ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+13 piercing plus 1d12 poison **Melee** claw +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d8+13 slashing **Melee** tail +21 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d12+13 bludgeoning **Melee** horn +21 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 1d12+11 piercing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 29 - **1st** [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=143) (at will) - **2nd** [Obscuring Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=210) **Breath Weapon** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) Ixamè breathes a cloud of poison like that emitted from Terwa Lake that deals 11d6 poison damage in a 40- foot cone (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). She can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. **Cloud Walk** Ixamè can tread on clouds or fog as though on solid ground. **Draconic Frenzy** Ixamè makes two claw Strikes and one horn Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** Ixamè recharges her Breath Weapon whenever she scores a critical hit when making a Strike.
name: Ixamè

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Bharlen Sajor](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1683) **Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 27 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +20, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +20 **Str** +5 **Dex** +7 **Con** +7 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Items** _+1 [breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10)_, heavy crossbow (20 bolts and 2 storm bolts [as [storm arrows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=169)]), [Trident](/Weapons.aspx?ID=45) --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +24 **Will** +18 **HP** 190 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When Bharlen is destroyed, her armor rebuilds her body over the course of 1d10 days—or more quickly if the armor is worn by a living host (see [Graveknight Armor](/Monsters.aspx?ID=244)). If the body is destroyed before then, the process restarts. Bharlen can only be permanently destroyed by obliterating her armor (such as with [_disintegrate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=76)), transporting it to the [Positive Energy Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=3), or throwing it into the heart of a volcano.
**Sacrilegious Aura** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64)) 30 feet. When a creature in the aura uses a [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) spell or ability, Bharlen automatically attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) it, with a +18 counteract modifier.
**No Escape** **Trigger** A creature within Bharlen's reach moves away from her; **Effect** Bharlen Strides up to her Speed, following the foe and keeping it in reach throughout this movement. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _shock trident_ +24 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)), **Damage** 3d8+11 piercing plus 1d6 electricity **Melee** fist +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d6+11 bludgeoning plus 1d6 electricity and Push **Ranged** _shock heavy crossbow_ +24 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 2](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 2d10+8 piercing plus 1d6 electricity **Ranged** _shock trident_ +24 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 3d8+11 piercing plus 1d6 electricity **Brute Strength** Bharlen deals an extra 1d8 damage with [tridents](/Weapons.aspx?ID=45), and a trident gains the [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193) trait while she is using it in melee (both the extra damage die and the shove trait have been included in Bharlen's trident Strikes above). **Devastating Blast** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) Bharlen unleashes crackling lightning in a 30-foot cone. Creatures in the area take 6d12 electricity damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Bharlen can use this ability once every 1d4 rounds. **Graveknight's Curse** This curse affects anyone who wears a graveknight's armor for at least 1 hour. **Saving Throw** DC 40 Will save; **Onset** 1 hour; **Stage 1** [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) and cannot remove the armor (1 day); **Stage 2** doomed 2, hampered 10, and cannot remove the armor (1 day); **Stage 3** dies and transforms into the armor's graveknight. **Ruinous Weapons** Any weapons Bharlen wields gain the effects of the _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=281) [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280)_ and _[shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303)_ runes. **Sodden Strike** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) Bharlen calls to the endless storm to lend murderous power to her blows. She makes a Strike; on a hit, seawater appears in the target's lungs and the target is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (sickened 3 on a critical hit). **Weapon Master** Bharlen has access to the [critical specialization effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235) of any weapon she wields.
name: Bharlen Sajor

**Speed** 20 feet, swim 25 feet; swamp stride

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
sickle +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tongue +20 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 bludgeoning and Tongue Grab

**Primal Spontaneous Spells** DC 28, attack +20
- **Cantrips (5th)**
[Acid Splash](/Spells.aspx?ID=3), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Tanglefoot](/Spells.aspx?ID=330)
- **1st**
[Fleet Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=122), [Grease](/Spells.aspx?ID=140), [Mending](/Spells.aspx?ID=189) (3 slots)
- **2nd**
[Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59), [Water Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=371), [Web](/Spells.aspx?ID=374) (3 slots)
- **3rd**
[Animal Vision](/Spells.aspx?ID=12), [Glyph of Warding](/Spells.aspx?ID=138), [Stinking Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=309) (3 slots)
- **4th**
[Acid Arrow](/Spells.aspx?ID=2), [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128), [Summon Elemental](/Spells.aspx?ID=320) (3 slots)
- **5th**
[Cloudkill](/Spells.aspx?ID=42), [Hydraulic Torrent](/Spells.aspx?ID=155) (2 slots)

**Rituals** DC 28

- **2nd**
[Consecrate](/Rituals.aspx?ID=8) (2nd)
- **6th**
[Primal Call](/Rituals.aspx?ID=19) (6th)

**Soul-Shaking Croak** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) Grouloop utters a powerful croak that deals 6d10 sonic damage to any non-[boggard](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=16) within a 30-foot emanation (DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). A creature with the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition that fails its saving throw increases its frightened condition by 1 (on a critical failure, the creature is also [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) for as long as it is frightened). Grouloop can't use Soul-Shaking Croak again for 1d4 rounds.

**Swamp Stride**   Grouloop ignores difficult terrain caused by swamp terrain features.

**Tongue Grab**   If Grouloop hits a creature with her tongue, that creature becomes [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20). Unlike with a normal grab, the creature isn't [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), but it can't move beyond the reach of Grouloop's tongue. A creature can sever the tongue by hitting AC 24 and dealing at least 9 slashing damage. Though this doesn't deal any damage to the boggard, it prevents her from using her tongue Strike until she regrows her tongue, which takes a week.

</column>
name: Grouloop
