Necrohusks are skittering, undead monstrosities, created when a humanoid is purposefully twisted into a new creature through necromantic experimentation. Unlike fleshwarps and recipients of successful necrografts, prospective necrohusks never survive the procedure. After reanimation, they're cunning, animalistic creatures that follow their creator's orders without question.
Creating a necrohusk is an incredibly difficult endeavor, fraught with error. Most attempts result in lumps of useless, rotten slurry or malformed undead no more cunning or dangerous than a mundane zombie. The costs in time, experimental subjects, and expensive spell components lead some would-be necromancers to conclude that the results aren't worth the risk. Occasionally, spellcasters can harness the power of the Negative Energy Plane during the process, which lessens the monetary cost but vastly increases the danger, often producing a deadly backlash resulting in the creator's death and a masterless necrohusk.
Among the Sutaki, only Ashen Swale and his lieutenants, Turkek and Azi, have the skill and knowledge to create necrohusks with any regularity, although Turkek's interests often lead him away from such gruesome endeavors. Necrohusks are created from volunteers in Ashen Swale's cult or from traitorous Sutaki whom Ashen Swale wants to punish with a ghastly fate. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 25 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 [Necrohusk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1778) **Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 87 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Hallit](/Languages.aspx?ID=37), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); can't speak **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** -3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -1 --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +15 **Will** +12 **HP** 75 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Skitter** **Trigger** A creature misses the necrohusk with a melee Strike; **Effect** The necrohusk Steps. --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 30 feet **Melee** jaws +15, **Damage** 2d6+5 piercing plus 1d6 poison **Melee** leg +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d4+5 piercing **Ranged** ichor spit +14 ([range 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d8 poison and vile innards **Frenzied Assault** The necrohusk makes three leg Strikes. If more than one Strike hits the same target, combine the damage of the Strikes that hit the creature and apply the creature's resistances and weaknesses only once. **Impaling Lunge** The necrohusk Strides twice. If the necrohusk ends its movement within melee range of an enemy, it makes a leg Strike against that enemy. On a hit, the Strike deals an additional 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). **Vile Innards** The necrohusk spews some of its rotting insides when it makes an ichor spit Strike. On a critical hit, the target is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) 2.
Further Experimentation Necrohusks range from Small to Large and often have alternative methods of locomotion and additional abilities. Suitable abilities from other undead creatures include bleeding and fiendish from [beheaded](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=220), bloody and explosive death from [skeletons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=92), and feast and rotting aura from [zombies](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=103). A necrohusk with additional abilities should be higher level than one without. Use the guidelines in Chapter 2 of the Gamemastery Guide to determine its new statistics.
name: Necrohusk

**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 28 **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Bargott](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1780) **Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 33 **Perception** +9; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +9, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +11, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +9 **Str** +3 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +11 **Will** +7 **HP** 70 **Immunities** [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effects **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5 --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** blighted branch +11 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 1d10+7 bludgeoning plus draining impact **Melee** claw +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 1d8+7 slashing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 17 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Tanglefoot](/Spells.aspx?ID=330) - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59), [Shatter](/Spells.aspx?ID=279) - **3rd** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110) - **Constant (4th)** [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **Draining Impact** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) When Bargott hits a creature with his blighted branch Strike, the creature must attempt a DC 20 Fortitude save. Whatever the result, the creature is immune to Bargott's draining impact for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) 1.
**Critical Failure** The creature is [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) 2. **Impart Anguish** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) Bargott twists his face into a visage of utter agony, sharing his anguish with those who gaze upon him. Creatures in a 15-foot cone take 3d6 mental damage (DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save). On a critically failed save, the creature is also [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) 1 for 1 round
name: Bargott

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 33 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 [Turkek](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1784) **Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 39 **Perception** +12; bloodsense (imprecise) 30 feet, [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Hallit](/Languages.aspx?ID=37), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +14, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13 **Str** +3 **Dex** +2 **Con** +2 **Int** +4 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +1 **Items** spellbook with all prepared spells, [wand of widening](/Equipment.aspx?ID=373) (2nd-level [_darkness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=59)) --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +16 **Will** +14 +2 circumstance to all saves vs. [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) **HP** 100 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Sanguine Aura** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [aura](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=5), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) 30 feet. Around Turkek, wounds heal slowly and blood flows faster. The flat check to stop [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) is DC 16, rather than DC 15. In addition, each time a creature in the emanation takes persistent bleed damage, it takes 1d6 additional bleed damage.
**Clot** **Trigger** Turkek takes persistent bleed damage **Effect** Turkek's blood hardens, sealing her wounds. Turkek automatically passes her next flat check to recover from the triggering [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fangs +16 ([Grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=184), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)), **Damage** 1d6+5 piercing plus 1d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 25, attack +17 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **1st** [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=141), [Mage Armor](/Spells.aspx?ID=176), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244) (×2) - **2nd** [False Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=108), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164), [Spider Climb](/Spells.aspx?ID=299) - **3rd** [Bind Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=22), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=141) (×2) - **4th** [Blood Vendetta](/Spells.aspx?ID=675), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=141), [Sanguine Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=989) **Blood Let** **Requirements** Turkek's last action was a fang Strike that hit the target; **Effect** Turkek rips her fangs across one of the target's arteries or veins, dealing an additional 1d6+5 slashing damage. **Siphon Blood** **Requirements** A creature within Turkek's sanguine aura is taking [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29); **Effect** Turkek draws her enemy's blood into her own body to heal her wounds. She regains 7 Hit Points.
name: Turkek

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Varilyn "Vare" Eridge](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1785) **Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 10 **Perception** +14 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +14, [Scouting Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +12 **Str** +4 **Dex** +1 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Guide's Warning** When Vare is about to roll a Perception or [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) check to determine her initiative, she can alert her allies as a free action. Vare visually or audibly warns allies, granting them a +1 circumstance bonus to their initiative rolls. Depending on how Vare warns allies, this action has the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) or [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) trait.
**Snare Crafting** Vare can use the [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) skill to create the following snares: [alarm snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=328), [hampering snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=336), [irritating thorn snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1519), [marking snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=339), [signaling snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=342), and [smoke screen snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1520). Vare can create four snares each day without paying for the materials, using 3 Interact actions to set up each snare. **Items** [Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=67) (20 bolts), [Scale Mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=8), [low-grade cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) [shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43) --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +11 **Will** +14 **HP** 60 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** shortsword +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d6+6 piercing **Ranged** crossbow +9 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d8+2 piercing **Guiding Words** Vare points out a weakness of a creature within 30 feet. Vare and all allies that can hear or see her gain a +1 status bonus to attack and damage rolls against that creature until the beginning of Vare's next turn. Vare adds an extra 1d6 precision damage to her weapon and unarmed attacks that hit that creature for the duration.
name: Varilyn "Vare" Eridge

<title level="1" pfs="">[Ashen Swale](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1791)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Ashen Swale's past is shrouded in mystery. He was born among his Sutaki brethren, but violent voices no one else could hear drew him to the wilds of Lost Mammoth Valley. In these wilds, he discovered his spirit guide Idovik and the art of necromancy. He returned to Lyuba a changed man. With Idovik at his side, he established new customs and foul rituals that further cemented the white dragon Venexus's reign over his people.

_spear_ +14 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)),
**Damage** 1d6+4 piercing

**Arcane Spontaneous Spells** DC 25, attack +17
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Electric Arc](/Spells.aspx?ID=97), [Forbidding Ward](/Spells.aspx?ID=126), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171)
- **3rd**
[Bind Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=22), [Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=26), [Envenom Companion](/Spells.aspx?ID=906), [Vampiric Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=354)
- **4th**
[Animate Dead](/Spells.aspx?ID=666), [Bone Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=1097), [Boneshaker](/Spells.aspx?ID=1098), [Enervation](/Spells.aspx?ID=687)

**Summoner Conduit Spells** DC 25, 1 Focus Point
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Boost Eidolon](/Spells.aspx?ID=1045), [Reinforce Eidolon](/Spells.aspx?ID=1050)

**Rituals** DC 25

- **2nd**
[Create Undead](/Rituals.aspx?ID=10)

**Corpse  Puppet** <actions string="Two Actions" />  ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32),  [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) Ashen Swale seizes control of the body of a creature within 30 feet that is dead or [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38). The target must attempt a Will save. Dead creatures automatically fail this save. On a success, the target is permanently immune to Corpse Puppet. On a failure, the target is controlled by Ashen Swale. It gains the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=109) trait and can act as though it were alive and conscious as long as it's controlled by Ashen Swale. Controlling each target requires Ashen Swale to take an action to Command a Minion. The only actions the target can take during this time are [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=80), [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=88), and [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=89). Ashen Swale retains control of the target for 10 minutes. This control ends immediately if the target loses the unconscious condition or the target is dealt an additional 50 damage since Ashen Swale took control (which destroys the body completely). After he has lost control of a target, that target is permanently immune to Corpse Puppet. Ashen Swale can control up to two targets with Corpse Puppet at a time.

**Hasten Eidolon** <actions string="Single Action" />  ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) Ashen Swale focuses on the link he shares with Idovik to quicken his eidolon's actions. Idovik is [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) 1 until the beginning of Ashen Swale's next turn.

**Manifest Eidolon** <actions string="Three Actions" />  ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [manipulate](/Actions.aspx?ID=639), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) Ashen Swale summons Idovik in an adjacent open space. If Idovik was already manifested, Ashen Swale chooses whether to unmanifest Idovik or to teleport Idovik to an adjacent open space. If Idovik unmanifested at 0 Hit Points, this process takes 1 minute instead of 3 actions, but Idovik regains 1 Hit Point when he remanifests.

</column>
name: Ashen Swale

<title level="1" pfs="">[Idovik](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1792)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Ashen Swale's past is shrouded in mystery. He was born among his Sutaki brethren, but violent voices no one else could hear drew him to the wilds of Lost Mammoth Valley. In these wilds, he discovered his spirit guide Idovik and the art of necromancy. He returned to Lyuba a changed man. With Idovik at his side, he established new customs and foul rituals that further cemented the white dragon Venexus's reign over his people.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 33

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Idovik](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1792)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="CN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=20" />
<trait label="Eidolon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=386" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 89

**Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Hallit](/Languages.aspx?ID=37), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet (Ashen Swale only)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +11, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +17, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +14

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +0

**Con** +4

**Int** +2

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +2
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 24 

**Fort** +17 

**Ref** +11 

**Will** +15 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 125

</row>

**God's Intervention** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Trigger** Ashen Swale is the target of a physical Strike <%END> **Effect** Sensing Ashen Swale's peril, Idovik teleports to his side and interposes himself between Ashen Swale and danger. Idovik teleports to a space adjacent to Ashen Swale, then shields Ashen Swale with his body. Idovik becomes the target of the triggering Strike.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tusk +18 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 2d10+8 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hoof +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 2d8+8 bludgeoning

**Independent Action**   Idovik is even older and more powerful than most Sarkorian gods, and he knows how to manifest in some forms without a god caller at all. Idovik does not share an initiative, actions, Hit Points, or a multiple attack penalty with Ashen Swale.

**Indomitable Step**   Idovik ignores difficult terrain and uneven ground, and he treats greater difficult terrain as normal difficult terrain.

**Otherworldly  Roar** <actions string="Two Actions" />  ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16))  Idovik unleashes a powerful squeal while stomping his hooves and displaying his tusks. Idovik attempts [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) each foe within 30 feet. These checks take no penalty for not sharing a language.

**Rampaging Charge** <actions string="Two Actions" />  Idovik rushes forward, using his momentum to power his attack. Idovik Strides twice and then makes a tusk Strike. As long as he moved at least 20 feet, he gains a +2 circumstance bonus to his attack roll. A Medium or smaller creature struck by this attack must succeed at a DC 25 Reflex save or be knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) by the force of the blow.

**Spirit Link**   Even if his link is unusual, Idovik is bound to his summoner; he must remain within 100 feet of Ashen Swale at all times. If he ever moves outside this range or is reduced to 0 Hit Points, Idovik's physical form dissolves. Because his link functions unusually and he does not share Hit Points with Ashen Swale, Idovik remains manifested when Ashen Swale is unconscious or dead, as long as he stays near Ashen Swale's body. If Ashen Swale dies, Idovik's form fades away over the next minute. When unmanifested, Idovik must be manifested before he can reappear.

</column>
name: Idovik

markdown:
The white dragon Venexus hatched in Winterwall Glacier, north of the Realm of the Mammoth Lords and Irrisen, alongside her brother Fezerod. Upon reaching maturity, Venexus and Fezerod migrated south to the Realm of the Mammoth Lords in search of unclaimed territory. Although the pair remained together for centuries, shortly after they conquered Lost Mammoth Valley together Venexus drove him off. The valley was, at the time, a highly frequented migratory route rich in resources, wildlife, and humanoids to subjugate. Fezerod fled in heartbreak and shame. In the centuries since, Venexus has maintained control of Lost Mammoth Valley, considering the region and everything within her property. The opening of the Worldwound to the east forever changed Lost Mammoth Valley and Venexus's role as its self-appointed ruler. Invading demons forced the Burning Mammoth following to abandon most of their ancient migratory route, including Lost Mammoth Valley, leaving Venexus without intelligent creatures to subjugate. Thankfully, it wasn't long before a group of Sarkorian refugees settled in the valley. After these people, who called themselves Sutaki, established a permanent settlement, she made herself known as their overlord. To ensure their cooperation, she built her glacial palace alongside them, blocking escape from the valley. Years later, a brave but foolish Burning Mammoth warrior named Metuak came to Lost Mammoth Valley seeking Venexus's aid. He asked her to end the curse placed upon him by the Primordial Flame and to free him of his possession by the shadow demon Xeleria. Rather than help, Venexus stole the Primordial Flame, forcing Metuak to flee for Hillcross. Although Venexus would have followed to ensure his death, she hadn't truly understood the artifact's curse or its powers. The lamp burned her badly and melted her palace, but she couldn't bear to part with it. It became, in time, both her most valuable asset and greatest weakness. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Venexus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1793) **Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 91 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 60 feet, storm vision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Hallit](/Languages.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +19, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +15 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **Storm Vision** Inclement weather doesn't impair Venexus's vision she ignores [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) from fog, mist, rain, and snowfall. **Items** [Primordial Flame](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1513) --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +15 **Will** +17 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 170 **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Frightful Presence** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 90 feet, DC 25
**Mist Shroud** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) 10 feet. The _Primordial Flame_ continually heats the cold emanating from Venexus's body, surrounding the dragon in a thin veil of fog. Creatures in the aura are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4), and other creatures are concealed to creatures in the area. Venexus's storm vision allows her to see through this concealment, however. If wind disperses the aura, it returns automatically at the start of Venexus's turn. The aura is suppressed in water.
**Freezing Blood** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) **Trigger** An adjacent creature deals piercing or slashing damage to Venexus <%END>) **Effect** Venexus's blood splatters onto the triggering creature, dealing 2d6 cold damage. A creature that takes cold damage in this way is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) 1 for 1 round. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 120 feet; ice climb 30 feet **Melee** jaws +21 ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+10 piercing plus 1d6 cold **Melee** claw +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d8+10 slashing **Melee** tail +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6+8 bludgeoningMelee tail +20 **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 25 - **1st** [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=143) (at will) - **2nd** [Obscuring Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=210) (at will) **Primordial Flame Spells** DC 25 - **5th** [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236) (at will) - **7th** [Sunburst](/Spells.aspx?ID=326) **Breath Weapon** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) Venexus breathes a cloud of frost that deals 10d6 cold damage in a 40-foot cone (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). She can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. **Draconic Frenzy** Venexus makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** When Venexus scores a critical hit with a Strike, she recharges her Breath Weapon. **Ice Climb** Venexus can climb on ice as though she had the listed climb Speed. She ignores difficult terrain and greater difficult terrain from ice and snow and doesn't risk falling when crossing ice. **Shape Ice** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) Venexus magically reshapes a cube of ice or snow she touches, up to 10 feet across. Any creature standing atop the ice must succeed at a DC 15 Reflex save or [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check. On a failure, the creature falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) on the ice; on a critical failure, it falls off the ice entirely and is also prone. **Snuff** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) Venexus releases a wave of sleet, which douses open flames within 30 feet. All non-magical fire is automatically extinguished. All creatures in the area suffering from persistent fire damage immediately attempt a DC 10 flat check to end the persistent damage. If Snuff overlaps with an ongoing [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) effect, Snuff attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the fire effect.
name: Venexus

Short-lived, strange creatures called alapolos cling to the shadows in eerie moonlight, inviting others to dance. Although these “shadow dancers” aren't actively malevolent, they become petulant when others won't dance with them. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Alapolo](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1794) **Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 80 **Perception** +28; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +30, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +30, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +30, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +30, [Shadow Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +30, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +36 **Str** +0 **Dex** +9 **Con** +5 **Int** +0 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +9 --- **AC** 41 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +34 **Will** +29 **HP** 310 **Immunities** [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=143) (see one with shadows) **One with Shadows** The alapolo isn't affected by [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), or [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=143) effects that target it unless it chooses to be. It can choose the degree of its saving throw result against darkness, light, or shadow effects that require it to make a saving throw.
**Cloying Pull** **Trigger** A creature within the alapolo's reach attempts to move away from the alapolo; **Effect** The triggering creature must succeed at a DC 36 Reflex save or its movement ends before it leaves the alopolo's reach. --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** shadow claw +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+15 slashing damage **Enticing Invitation** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The alapolo whispers an invitation to a creature within 30 feet. The creature must attempt a DC 38 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and temporarily immune to all Enticing Invitations for 24 hours.
**Success** The target must spend 1 action on its next turn dancing in place.
**Failure** The target becomes [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). It is also [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) by the alapolo for 1 minute and can't attack the alapolo as long as it's fascinated. While fascinated, the target must spend 2 actions on each of its turns dancing in place.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the target is drained 3 and must spend 3 actions on each of its turns dancing in place. **Shifting Shadows** An alapolo's movement doesn't trigger reactions. **Weighty Shadow** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Requirements** The alapolo is adjacent to a target creature, or the GM determines the alapolo is adjacent to that creature's shadow; **Effect** The alapolo petulantly stomps on a target's shadow, making it incredibly heavy to drag around. The creature takes a –20-foot penalty to its Speeds for 1 minute. As normal, this penalty can't reduce a creature's Speed below 5 feet. Any creatures fascinated by the alapolo due to its Enticing Invitation are freed from their fascination when the alapolo uses this ability, jarred by the graceless stomping.
Shadow Dancers Even fey avoid alapolos, for their dancing is both captivating and exhausting. When a potential dance partner resists their swaying invitations to dance, an alapolo becomes sulky and usually stomps upon the refuser's shadow, giving it a supernatural weight. Avoiding both the dance and the stomp requires incredible diplomacy.
name: Alapolo

Clockworks are machines built by engineers and augmented with magic. A clock tower reconfigured as an animated clockwork can rotate slices of time. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 45 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 43 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 [Clockwork Clock Tower](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1795) **Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 81 **Perception** +34; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +38 **Str** +10 **Dex** +6 **Con** +7 **Int** -5 **Wis** +6 **Cha** -5 **Wind-Up** 1 week, DC 40 --- **AC** 48 **Fort** +36 **Ref** +33 **Will** +31 **HP** 325 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 20 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) or orichalcum) **Weaknesses** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 20, [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=378) 20 **Durable Exterior** When a clockwork clock tower is reduced to fewer than 200 HP or is damaged by a critical hit, its exterior shatters to reveal internal mechanisms, reducing its AC to 44 until it recovers enough HP to reach 200 HP or more.
**Doleful Tolling** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93)) **Trigger** The clockwork clock tower rolls initiative; **Effect** The clock tower chimes, and all non-clockwork creatures within 300 feet who can hear it must succeed at a DC 42 Will save or be [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round (slowed 2 on a critical failure). --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** fist +36 ([reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d12+18 bludgeoning plus stutter time **Melee** foot +36 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+18 bludgeoning **Overclock** The clockwork clock tower loses 1 day from its winding time and becomes [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) for 3 rounds. It can use this extra action to Step, Stride, or Strike. This quickened condition ends immediately if the clock tower is damaged by [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=378). **Stutter Time** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) A creature struck by a clockwork clock tower's fist must make a DC 40 Will save as time flows unevenly around it.
**Critical Success** The creature is [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) for 1 round and can use this extra action to Step, Stride, or Strike.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [stunned 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
**Critical Failure** The creature is knocked out of time, which ceases to flow around the creature for 1 round. It is invulnerable to all damage, it can't be targeted or affected by anything, and no rounds elapse for any timed durations, conditions, afflictions, and other effects it has. The target can't act and remains fixed in place, defying gravity if applicable. After time begins to flow again for it, the creature is stunned 3. **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Huge or smaller, foot, DC 42
Enemies Across Time Engineers who develop clockwork clock towers often draw unwelcome attention from creatures who protect the flow of time and don't like mortal machines meddling with it. These inventors might be attacked by [hounds of Tindalos](/Monsters.aspx?ID=702) or even [ravagers of Tindalos](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1717).
name: Clockwork Clock Tower

<title level="1" pfs="">[Crawling Slurry](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1796)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Acid damage can scar stone and reduce flesh to pitted chunks, but some organic compounds are resistant to acid and respond by combining into a mass of roiling, living slurry. These scarred clumps hunger to consume anything they can sense. They move with quick, darting contractions that surprise those used to facing slow-moving oozes.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Ooze](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 40

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Ooze_CrawlingSlurry.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 16">[Crawling Slurry](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1796)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Acid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=3" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=108" />
<trait label="Ooze" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=122" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 82

**Perception** +26; motion sense 60 feet, no vision

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +30

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +9

**Con** +6

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** -5
</row>

**Motion Sense** A crawling slurry can sense nearby motion through vibration and air movement.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 30 

**Fort** +28 

**Ref** +31 

**Will** +20 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 300

</row>

**Immunities**
[acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), critical hits, [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)

**Splatter** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The crawling slurry takes bludgeoning damage from a creature it can sense within 10 feet; **Effect** The crawling slurry ejects a splash of acid onto the creature, dealing acid damage equal to the bludgeoning damage dealt to the crawling slurry (DC 37 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, climb 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
pseudopod +32,
**Damage** 3d10+11 bludgeoning plus 2d8 acid and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** <actions string="Single Action" />  2d10+3 bludgeoning plus 2d8 acid, DC 37

**Crawling Slurry Acid**   A crawling slurry's acid damages metals and organic materials but not stone.

**[Engulf](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=14)** <actions string="Two Actions" />  DC 37, 5d10 acid, [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC 37, Rupture 20. A crawling slurry regains 30 Hit Points if a living creature it is engulfing dies.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Accidental Oozes</title>

Crawling slurries are almost always by-products of toxic waste or alchemical runoff into areas with a high concentration of acid, magic, or both. The first quickening pulses of animation in acid-pitted flesh are easy enough to end with counteracting chemicals or even isolation and starvation, but most crawling slurries are left to fester for a long time and become as large as a person. Left untended in a vat of nutritive acids and organic matter, a crawling slurry might become truly immense, but the power such a slithering horror holds is beyond comprehension.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-79" />
name: Crawling Slurry

Chromatic oozes result from the worst kind of alchemical mishaps: attempts to combine as many types of elemental damage as possible into a single alchemical substance. Although the source of these alchemical concatenations are as varied as the alchemists who attempt to distill them, one of the most common methods for creating them involves combining the essences of multiple types of chromatic dragons together using eldritch alchemical processes. When this type of experimentation goes dangerously wrong—as it often does—the result is a combination of terrible elemental power with the mindless destructive power of an ooze. A chromatic ooze is a rippling, seething substance that shifts through several different colors, but chemical reactions within the ooze can lock its energy and its color into a specific phase for a short time. Whether a chromatic ooze's destructive urges derive from the frustrated ingenuity of an alchemist creator or from the hunger of the chromatic dragons that so often constitute these oozes' forms, the best way to deal with a chromatic ooze is to stay out of its way. **[Recall Knowledge - Ooze](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 43 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 41 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 [Chromatic Ooze](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1797) **Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 81 **Perception** +30; motion sense 240 feet, no vision **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35 **Str** +9 **Dex** -5 **Con** +10 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 **Motion Sense** A chromatic ooze can sense nearby motion through vibration and air movement. --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +33 **Ref** +16 **Will** +20 **HP** 550 **Immunities** critical hits, [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) **Caustic Aura** ([alchemical](/Traits.aspx?ID=6), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246)) 90 feet. The chromatic ooze emanates noxious chemicals into the environment. A creature that enters the aura or starts its turn within it is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) unless it succeeds at a DC 37 Fortitude save. Creatures with the [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) ability or a particularly heightened sense of smell are sickened 2, or sickened 1 on a successful Fortitude save. Creatures in the aura can't recover from the sickened condition.
**Chromatic Shift** **Trigger** The chromatic ooze takes acid, cold, electricity, fire, or poison damage; **Effect** The chromatic ooze changes its color based on the triggering damage (to dark green, pale blue, dark blue, red, or light green, respectively) and gains resistance 20 to the triggering damage. If it already had resistance to another damage type from this ability, it loses the resistance to that damage type and gains weakness 20 to it. If it already had weakness to another damage type from this ability, it loses that weakness. After 1 minute, the chromatic ooze returns to its usual rippling, multicolored hue and loses the resistance and weakness from this ability. --- **Speed** 20 feet, climb 20 feet, swim 20 feet **Melee** pseudopod +35 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+17 bludgeoning plus chromatic slam and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Chromatic Slam** The ooze's pseudopod Strikes deal an additional 2d10 damage of the same type it resists from its Chromatic Shift ability, if any. **[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=19)** 3d10+13 bludgeoning, DC 40 **Pseudopod Eruption** The chromatic ooze concentrates its mass into several pseudopods, increasing its [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) to 30 feet until the end of its turn. It then Strikes at up to four different creatures within its reach. This counts as two attacks for the ooze's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after it makes all the attacks.
Dangers of Hubris Spend enough time among alchemists and you'll likely hear tales of bombs capable of producing multiple elemental effects at the same time. The process for making these bombs requires specialized ingredients and the utmost skill. Few alchemists manage to combine two damage types, much less five. These the latter efforts, when mishandled, create chromatic oozes.
name: Chromatic Ooze

<title level="1" pfs="">[Slana](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1798)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The rolling thunder before the rain provides the massive, leonine humanoids known as slanas with a tremendous power. These solitary and nomadic creatures primarily dwell in the jungles and savannas of the Mwangi Expanse, but they can range into nearby areas in search of prey or when drawn by strange primal forces only slanas can recognize. Slanas view each other as rivals who test each other's prowess, so when drawn together by this strange calling they nearly always fight to the death. A slana's roar echoes like thunder across the countryside, and denizens of the Mwangi fear a slana's approach whenever an oncoming storm's thunder seems more ferocious than its rain.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 45

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 43

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Slana.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 20">[Slana](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1798)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="CN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Gargantuan" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 84

**Perception** +36; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 120 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +35, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +38, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +35, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +33, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +37

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +10

**Dex** +7

**Con** +7

**Int** +2

**Wis** +7

**Cha** +6
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 45 

**Fort** +35 

**Ref** +33 

**Will** +33 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 380
([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) 20)

</row>

**Immunities**
[frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19)

**Resistances**
[sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147) 30

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 60 feet, climb 30 feet, swim 30 feet; [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)),
**Damage** 4d10+20 piercing plus 2d8 sonic and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)),
**Damage** 4d6+20 piercing plus 2d8 sonic

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
thunderclap +35 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)),
**Damage** thunder trap

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 40, attack +32
- **1st**
[Obscuring Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=210) (at will)
- **9th**
[Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Storm Lord](/Spells.aspx?ID=477) (×3)
- **10th**
[Earthquake](/Spells.aspx?ID=95), [Storm of Vengeance](/Spells.aspx?ID=313)
- **Constant (4th)**
[Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128)

**Great Roar** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) The slana emits a deafening roar. All creatures within a 100-foot cone take 16d8 sonic damage (DC 44 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex saving throw). On a failed save, a creature is also [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) for 1 minute and [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) (or deafened permanently and stunned 3 on a critical failure). The slana can't use Great Roar again for 1d4 rounds.

**Pounce** <actions string="Single Action" />  The slana Strides and makes a Strike at the end of that movement. If the slana began this action [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), they remain hidden until after the ability's Strike.

**Thunder Trap**   A creature hit by the slana's thunderclap attack is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) and held in place by reverberations of sound until it [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) or [Forces Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34) the bonds of thunder that constrain it (DC 44). The target is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) as long as it is immobilized by a thunder trap.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">The Thunder Lion and the Rain</title>

The hours before an immense storm are hours to be feared, for it is said that this is when the thunder lion hunts. No mere hare or ibex can slake his great hunger; only large and ferocious creatures like elephants or dinosaurs will suffice. The rain won't come until the thunder lion eats, as it is the spirit of its kill that releases such precipitation as it flees into the sky.
</aside>
name: Slana

markdown:
The [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10) still holds the first rough draft of many creatures in the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), but it's hard to tell what sort of creature the ta'apundo was ever intended to be. Its bark poses significant danger to metallic objects, as it converts any metal touching it into wood as fibrous and as flexible as its own body. Capricious and skittish, ta'apundos make friends easily but consider anyone who wields or wears metal to be a hated enemy. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 44 **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 44 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 [Ta'apundo](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1799) **Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 85 **Perception** +35; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), greensight **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +35, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +33, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +35, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +37 **Str** +6 **Dex** +10 **Con** +5 **Int** +0 **Wis** +10 **Cha** +6 **Greensight** Vegetation doesn't impair a ta'apundo's vision and doesn't provide a creature cover or [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) from a ta'apundo. --- **AC** 45 **Fort** +30 **Ref** +35 **Will** +33 **HP** 440 **Resistances** wood 20 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 20, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 20 **Wood Resistance** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) A ta'apundo has resistance 20 against wooden objects and unarmed attacks from creatures made mostly of wood (such as [arboreals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=8)).
**Lignification** ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Trigger** a non–[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272) metal object damages the ta'apundo; **Effect** The ta'apundo emits a jolt of magic into the object, transforming it into wood with an unlimited duration. A thrown weapon, ammunition, or other unattended item automatically transforms; the wielder of an attended object can resist this effect with a successful DC 41 Fortitude save. [_Dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) can end the effect. While a weapon is wood, the ta'apundo's resistance applies. Once transformed, a formerly metal item's Hardness and HP change to those of wood. This typically inflicts a –2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls made with metal weapons and a –2 circumstance penalty to AC for those wearing metal armor; the GM might determine a different penalty for a specific weapon or armor. --- **Speed** 20 feet; woodland stride **Melee** branch +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+12 bludgeoning **Branch Whip** The ta'apundo makes four branch Strikes against different targets, increasing its reach to 20 feet for these Strikes. These attacks count toward its multiple attack penalty, but this penalty doesn't increase until after all four attacks. **Woodland Stride** The ta'apundo ignores difficult and greater difficult terrain from foliage.
Seed Survivors [The Green Mother](/Deities.aspx?ID=93), one of the erratic fey deities known collectively as the Eldest, planted the seeds for several ta'apundos across the world long ago. With the devastation of Earthfall and the destruction of natural places across much of the world, most ta'apundo seeds were destroyed. In the Mwangi Expanse, however, many seeds took root. Most ta'apundos that exist on Golarion—which are far fewer than the number in the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10)—can be found in the Mwangi jungles.
name: Ta'apundo

Some wereants have a religious zeal about training to protect their community and gladly give their lives for the hive. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 40 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Wereant Disciple](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1800) **Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 86 **Perception** +28; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); insect empathy, [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 120 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +31, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +29, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +28 **Str** +6 **Dex** +9 **Con** +5 **Int** +0 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +4 **Hive Mind** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) Wereants operate with a shared hive intelligence. While within telepathic range of at least one other wereant, a wereant disciple gains a +2 circumstance bonus to initiative checks, Perception checks, and saving throws against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects. If one wereant is aware of a combatant, all members of the hive mind within telepathic range are aware of it.
**Insect Empathy** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The wereant can communicate with insects. --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +30 **Will** +25 **HP** 305 **Weaknesses** [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) 15 **Gnawing Retort** **Trigger** A creature adjacent to the wereant critically misses the wereant with a melee Strike; **Effect** The wereant makes a mandibles Strike against the triggering creature. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** mandibles +29, **Damage** 3d10+14 slashing plus curse of the wereant **Melee** carapace fist +32 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 3d8+14 bludgeoning plus staggering blow **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The wereant changes into their humanoid, hybrid, or animal shape. Each shape has a specific, persistent appearance. A true wereant's natural form is their hybrid shape. In humanoid shape, they use their original humanoid size, lose their jaws and claw Strikes, and gain a melee fist Strike that deals bludgeoning damage equal to the slashing damage dealt by their mandibles. In animal shape, they take the form of a Medium giant ant, gain a climb Speed of 20 feet, gain [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) on their mandibles Strike, and lose their weapon Strikes. **Curse of the Wereant** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) This curse affects only [humanoids](/Traits.aspx?ID=91); **Saving Throw** DC 34 Fortitude. On each full moon, the cursed creature must succeed at another Fortitude save or turn into a wereant until dawn. The creature is under the GM's control and goes on a rampage for half the night before falling [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) until dawn. **Flying Leap** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The wereant disciple Flies and makes two Strikes against different targets at any point during that movement. These Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage if the disciple is above the target. If the wereant began this action [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), they remain hidden until after this ability's Strikes. Both attacks count toward the disciple's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after both Strikes. **Moon Frenzy** ([Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) When a full moon appears in the night sky, the wereant must enter hybrid form, can't Change Shape, becomes one size larger, increases their reach by 5 feet, and increases the damage of their mandibles Strike by 2. When the moon sets or the sun rises, the wereant returns to humanoid form and is [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) for 2d4 hours. **Staggering Blow** A creature damaged by the wereant disciple's carapace fist Strike must succeed at a DC 35 Fortitude save or become [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) (stunned 2 on a critical failure). If the Strike is a critical hit, a creature that fails the save is also [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 1 round.
name: Wereant Disciple

When overwhelming force is inadvisable, a wereant hive sends a poison-dripping assassin for murderous work. These poisoners love to terrify those dying in agony from their toxins. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 41 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Wereant Poisoner](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1801) **Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 87 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); insect empathy, [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 120 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +33, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +30, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +30, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +32, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +30, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +30 **Str** +6 **Dex** +9 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +5 **Hive Mind** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) Wereants operate with a shared hive intelligence. While within telepathic range of at least one other wereant, a wereant disciple gains a +2 circumstance bonus to initiative checks, Perception checks, and saving throws against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects. If one wereant is aware of a combatant, all members of the hive mind within telepathic range are aware of it.
**Insect Empathy** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The wereant can communicate with insects. **Items** _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [kukri](/Weapons.aspx?ID=56)_ (2) --- **AC** 40 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +32 **Will** +29 **HP** 350 **Weaknesses** silver 15 **Gnawing Retort** **Trigger** A creature adjacent to the wereant critically misses the wereant with a melee Strike; **Effect** The wereant makes a mandibles Strike against the triggering creature. --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** mandibles +31, **Damage** 3d10+12 slashing plus 2d6 [persistent poison](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and curse of the wereant **Melee** kukri +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 3d6+12 slashing plus 2d6 [persistent poison](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Change Shape** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The wereant changes into their humanoid, hybrid, or animal shape. Each shape has a specific, persistent appearance. A true wereant's natural form is their hybrid shape. In humanoid shape, they use their original humanoid size, lose their jaws and claw Strikes, and gain a melee fist Strike that deals bludgeoning damage equal to the slashing damage dealt by their mandibles. In animal shape, they take the form of a Medium giant ant, gain a climb Speed of 20 feet, gain [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) on their mandibles Strike, and lose their weapon Strikes. **Curse of the Wereant** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) This curse affects only [humanoids](/Traits.aspx?ID=91); **Saving Throw** DC 34 Fortitude. On each full moon, the cursed creature must succeed at another Fortitude save or turn into a wereant until dawn. The creature is under the GM's control and goes on a rampage for half the night before falling [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) until dawn. **Envenomed Weapons** Weapons the wereant poisoner holds deal an additional 2d6 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) as poison seeps from the wereant's hands onto the weapon. Weapons cease to deal this persistent poison damage once the wereant lets them go. The kukri Strikes above include this persistent poison damage. **Moon Frenzy** ([Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) When a full moon appears in the night sky, the wereant must enter hybrid form, can't Change Shape, becomes one size larger, increases their reach by 5 feet, and increases the damage of their mandibles Strike by 2. When the moon sets or the sun rises, the wereant returns to humanoid form and is [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) for 2d4 hours. **Poisonous Flurry** The wereant poisoner makes two kukri Strikes and a mandibles Strike in any order. **Vitriolic Strikes** If the wereant critically hits a creature with a melee Strike, that creature can't recover from persistent poison damage for 1 round.
name: Wereant Poisoner

markdown:
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Lignified Adamantine Golem](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1802) **Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 16 **Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +36 **Str** +9 **Dex** -1 **Con** +9 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 40 **Fort** +31 **Ref** +25 **Will** +27 **HP** 255 (repair mode) **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), magic (see below), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 15 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374)) **Golem Antimagic** harmed by [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3) (9d10, 2d10 from areas or persistent damage); healed by [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) (area 2d10 HP); slowed by [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56)
**Repair Mode** When the golem is at 0 HP, it isn't destroyed. Instead, it enters repair mode, during which it is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35), can't take reactions, and can take only the Self-Repair action. Once it has more than 30 HP, it can use any type of action and can use reactions, though it remains slowed 1 and can't take any reactions until the start of its next turn. If a critical hit with an adamantine weapon reduces the golem to 0 HP, or if such a weapon hits it while it's already at 0 HP, then the golem is destroyed.
**Vulnerable to Dispelling and Shape Wood** The golem can be targeted by [_disjunction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=77), [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), and [_shape wood_](/Spells.aspx?ID=277). If targeted by such a spell of 9th level or higher, the golem has its resistance to physical damage lowered to 10 and is slowed 1 (or slowed 2 if in repair mode) for 1d4 rounds. During this time, if the golem is reduced to 0 HP while already in repair mode, it is destroyed. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** staff +33 ([deadly 3d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+15 bludgeoning plus destructive strike **Destructive Strike** On a critical hit, the golem's staff Strike breaks the target's armor, if any, in addition to dealing damage to the target. If the target has a shield raised, the golem breaks the shield instead. **Inexorable March** The golem Strides up to its Speed, pushing back each creature whose space it moves into and damaging them if they try to stop its movement. A creature can try to bar the way by attempting a DC 43 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature takes no damage and its armor takes no damage.
**Success** The golem halts its movement and cannot enter the creature's square.
**Failure** The resisting creature is damaged and its armor takes damage as if hit by the golem's staff. **Self-Repair** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) The golem repairs itself, regaining 30 Hit Points. **Vent Splinters** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) The golem vents a 30-foot cone of fiery splinters from its internal forge. This deals 13d6 piercing damage to all creatures in the cone (DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Creatures who fail the save also take 2d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) (4d6 persistent fire damage on a critical failure). The golem can't use Vent Splinters again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Lignified Adamantine Golem

markdown:
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 48 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 46 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 43 [Unshadowed Okoro](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1803) **Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 18 **Perception** +30 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Terran](/Languages.aspx?ID=22) **Skills** [Academia Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +33, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +31, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +35, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +35, [Games Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +31, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +31, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +35, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +29 **Str** +2 **Dex** +3 **Con** +3 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Items** _+3 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [composite shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75)_ (30 arrows) --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +30 **Ref** +27 **Will** +33 **HP** 270 **Resistances** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3) 20 **Shadow's Displeasure** When Okoro has fewer than 150 Hit Points, his shadow pulls at his essence to show the Vesicant Egg's displeasure. He looks pained and confused, and he becomes [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) until healed to 150 Hit Points or more. These conditions increase to 2 when below 100 Hit Points. When below 50 Hit Points, he falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38).
**Vesicated Shadow** Okoro's shadow has become corrupted via the power of the Vesicant Egg. His shredded shadow grants him some control over acid and shadows. He gains a +4 status bonus to resist [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3) and [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=143) effects. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)), **Damage** 3d4+6 bludgeoning **Ranged** _composite shortbow_ +28 ([deadly 2d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 3d6+22 piercing **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 40, attack +32 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=142), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **1st** [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=141), [Magic Missile](/Spells.aspx?ID=180), [Unseen Servant](/Spells.aspx?ID=352) (3 slots) - **2nd** [Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=28), [Hideous Laughter](/Spells.aspx?ID=150), [Sound Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=292) (3 slots) - **3rd** [Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=26), [Magic Missile](/Spells.aspx?ID=180), [Vampiric Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=354) (3 slots) - **4th** [Blink](/Spells.aspx?ID=27), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=141) (3 slots) - **5th** [Black Tentacles](/Spells.aspx?ID=23), [Magic Missile](/Spells.aspx?ID=180), [Synaptic Pulse](/Spells.aspx?ID=327) (3 slots) - **6th** [Collective Transposition](/Spells.aspx?ID=43), [Phantasmal Calamity](/Spells.aspx?ID=218), [Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=353) (3 slots) - **7th** [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=141), [Magic Missile](/Spells.aspx?ID=180), [Prismatic Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=233) (3 slots) - **8th** [Maze](/Spells.aspx?ID=187), [Prismatic Wall](/Spells.aspx?ID=234), [Spirit Song](/Spells.aspx?ID=303) (3 slots) - **9th** [Magic Missile](/Spells.aspx?ID=180), [Prismatic Sphere](/Spells.aspx?ID=232), [Telepathic Demand](/Spells.aspx?ID=336) (3 slots) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 40, attack +32 - **9th** [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=273) (acid only; ×2), [Shadow Siphon](/Spells.aspx?ID=274) (×2) **Observe Weakness** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [Fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Okoro studies a creature within 30 feet to find an opening to attack. On his next attack roll against the creature this turn, he rolls twice and uses the higher result, and his attack gains the [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) trait. The target becomes temporarily immune for 1d4 rounds. **Push My Luck** **Trigger** Okoro targets a creature with an [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15); **Requirements** The triggering creature was the target of Observe Weakness this turn; **Effect** Okoro draws upon his future luck to guarantee his attack. When he makes his attack roll, if he rolls lower than 10 on the die, he treats that attack roll as a 10 instead. This is in addition to rolling twice and taking the higher result from Observe Weakness. Afterward, Okoro's luck is temporarily spent, and he must roll twice and use the lower result of his next 3 checks; this is a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effect. He can't use Push My Luck again until he's completed these 3 checks. **Rupture Shadow** ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=143)) Okoro can create immense blisters on a creature's shadow, which immediately burst, dealing 20d6 acid damage. He targets a creature within 60 feet that is casting a shadow, which must attempt a DC 37 Will save. Okoro can't use Rupture Shadow again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune for 1 day.
**Success** The target takes half damage. The bursting shadow deals 5 acid [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage to all creatures within 5 feet of the target. **Failure** The target takes full damage, and the bursting shadow deals 10 acid splash damage.
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage, the bursting shadow deals 20 acid splash damage, and the target also takes 4d6 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)
name: Unshadowed Okoro

**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Thorn Guardian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1804) **Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 19 **Perception** +27; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +33 **Str** +6 **Dex** +9 **Con** +6 **Int** -5 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +30 **Will** +35 **HP** 330 **Immunities** critical hits, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Extra Reaction** A thorn guardian gains an additional reaction each turn but can use this extra reaction only to Retaliate.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Retaliate** **Trigger** A creature within the thorn guardian's reach attacks the guardian's ward; **Effect** The thorn guardian makes a briar Strike against the triggering creature. If it hits, it deals an additional 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). --- **Speed** fly 20 feet **Melee** briar +30, **Damage** 3d12+12 **Bond with Ward** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114)) **Requirements** The thorn guardian is adjacent to its ward; **Effect** The thorn guardian enters its ward's space and encases the creature. This doesn't interfere with its ward's senses or actions. The thorn guardian occupies the same space as its ward and moves with its ward.
While worn, the thorn guardian establishes a constant [_shield other_](/Spells.aspx?ID=281) effect with its ward. Attacks that target the thorn guardian target the ward instead but use the higher AC or saving throw values between the thorn guardian and the ward. Area effects apply to both the thorn guardian and the ward, as normal for an area effect. **Fly Free** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114)) **Requirements** The thorn guardian is Bonded with its Ward; **Effect** The thorn guardian removes itself from the ward and enters an adjacent space. **Interpose** **Requirements** The thorn guardian is Bonded with or adjacent to its ward; **Effect** The thorn guardian interposes itself between the ward and its foes, granting its ward a +2 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of the thorn guardian's next turn.
name: Thorn Guardian
