**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 48 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 46 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 43 [Unshadowed Mariama](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1805) **Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 20 **Perception** +30 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Gnoll](/Languages.aspx?ID=17), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Academia Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +35, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +31, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +29, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +35, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +31, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +31 **Str** +1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +30 **Ref** +27 **Will** +33 **HP** 270 **Resistances** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3) 20 **Shadow's Displeasure** When Mariama has fewer than 150 Hit Points, her shadow pulls at her essence to show the Vesicant Egg's displeasure. She looks pained and confused, and she becomes [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) until healed to 150 Hit Points or more. These conditions increase to 2 when below 100 Hit Points. When below 50 Hit Points, she falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38).
**Vesicated Shadow** Mariama's shadow has become corrupted via the power of the Vesicant Egg. Her shredded shadow grants her some control over acid and shadows. She gains a +4 status bonus to resist [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3) and [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=143) effects.
**Weird Astrology** Perhaps because of Mariama's quirky nature, she has received all the effects of the astrological evaluator. When she gains any of the clumsy, [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), or stupefied conditions, she reduces the value by 1. This means her Shadow's Displeasure doesn't affect her until she has fewer than 50 Hit Points, when she falls unconscious. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188)), **Damage** 3d4+4 bludgeoning **Occult Prepared Spells** DC 40, attack +32 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Forbidding Ward](/Spells.aspx?ID=126), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **1st** [Bane](/Spells.aspx?ID=18), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) - **2nd** [Death Knell](/Spells.aspx?ID=63), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164), [Paranoia](/Spells.aspx?ID=214) - **3rd** [Chilling Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=36), [Hypnotic Pattern](/Spells.aspx?ID=157), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213) - **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125) - **5th** [Hallucination](/Spells.aspx?ID=144), [Synaptic Pulse](/Spells.aspx?ID=327), [Synesthesia](/Spells.aspx?ID=328) - **6th** [Feeblemind](/Spells.aspx?ID=112), [Spellwrack](/Spells.aspx?ID=298), [Vibrant Pattern](/Spells.aspx?ID=357) - **7th** [Duplicate Foe](/Spells.aspx?ID=93), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=369) - **8th** [Disappearance](/Spells.aspx?ID=73), [Spiritual Epidemic](/Spells.aspx?ID=304), [Uncontrollable Dance](/Spells.aspx?ID=347) - **9th** [Foresight](/Spells.aspx?ID=127), [Synesthesia](/Spells.aspx?ID=328), [Unfathomable Song](/Spells.aspx?ID=349) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 40, attack +32 - **9th** [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=273) (acid only; ×2), [Shadow Siphon](/Spells.aspx?ID=274) (×2) **Witch Hexes** DC 40, 3 Focus Points - **Cantrips (9th)** [Nudge Fate](/Spells.aspx?ID=802) - **9th** [Blood Ward](/Spells.aspx?ID=791), [Cackle](/Spells.aspx?ID=792), [Curse of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=794), [Malicious Shadow](/Spells.aspx?ID=800) **Bind Destinies** ([Abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Mariama ties her fate with that of another creature within 30 feet. The creature must attempt a DC 40 Will save. Regardless of the result of its save, the creature is temporarily immune for 1 day.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The two fates are bound. Whenever Mariama takes damage, the creature takes damage of the same type equal to half the damage Mariama took (DC 37 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). Immunities, resistances, and weaknesses still apply as normal. The creature can attempt to sever the bond by spending an action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, to attempt another DC 40 Will save. On a success, the bond is severed, and the effect ends. The bond also ends when Mariama falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), or if she actively ends the effect herself as a free action.
**Failure** As success, but the creature takes damage equal to the full amount Mariama took. Severing the bond requires a 2-action activity, which has the concentrate trait.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the basic Fortitude save is DC 40. Severing the bond requires a 3-action activity, which has the concentrate trait. **Rupture Shadow** ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=143)) Mariama can create immense blisters on a creature's shadow, which immediately burst, dealing 20d6 acid damage. She targets a creature within 60 feet that is casting a shadow, which must attempt a DC 37 Will save. Mariama can't use Rupture Shadow again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune for 1 day.
**Success** The target takes half damage. The bursting shadow deals 5 acid [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage to all creatures within 5 feet of the target.
**Failure** The target takes full damage, and the bursting shadow deals 10 acid splash damage.
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage, the bursting shadow deals 20 acid splash damage, and the target also takes 4d6 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
name: Unshadowed Mariama

shadow whip +36 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)),
**Damage** 3d4+14 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27)

**Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 40, attack  +32
- **Cantrips (9th)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Electric Arc](/Spells.aspx?ID=97), [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246)

- **9th**
[Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Foresight](/Spells.aspx?ID=127), [Telepathic Demand](/Spells.aspx?ID=336)

**Arcane Innate Spells** DC 40
- **4th**
[Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59), [Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=94)

- **8th**
[Imprisonment](/Rituals.aspx?ID=13)

**Claim Trophy** <actions string="Single Action" />  Shianshi claims the soul of a creature she killed within the last minute. This works like [_bind soul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=21), except that no black sapphire is required and the soul is turned into a glowing blue light called a soul trophy. Anyone who kills Shianshi can then free the soul from any soul trophy by touching it and speaking the word for “freedom” in any language.

**Huntblade Brutality**   Shianshi's greatsword Strikes have the [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) trait and deal an additional 2d8 damage to [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16), or [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) creatures.

**Hunter's Leap** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Trigger** Shianshi kills a creature; **Effect** Shianshi teleports up to 30 feet and makes a greatsword Strike; on a hit, the target of the Strike is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (frightened 3 on a critical hit).

**Whip Drain** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Requirements** Shianshi has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) with her shadow whip; **Effect** The grabbed creature must succeed at a DC 41 Fortitude save or become [drained 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (drained 3 on a critical failure). If the creature is already drained, this increases its drained value instead, to a maximum of drained 4.

</column>
name: Shianshi Waymaker

He targets a creature within 60 feet that is casting a shadow, which must attempt a DC 37 Will save. Haibram can't use Rupture Shadow again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune for 1 day.
**Success** The target takes half damage. The bursting shadow deals 5 acid [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage to all creatures within 5 feet of the target.
**Failure** The target takes full damage, and the bursting shadow deals 10 acid splash damage.
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage, the bursting shadow deals 20 acid splash damage, and the target also takes 4d6 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). **Wind Step** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Haibram draws air around him to aid his movement. He Strides up to his Speed, gaining the effects of [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6) and ignoring difficult terrain while moving. He can increase the number of actions to 3 to Stride up to three times his Speed instead. Haibram begins to fall immediately after using Wind Step but treats the fall as 60 feet shorter thanks to the supporting winds. **Wind Touch** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) Haibram imbues a weapon he's holding with the power of the wind until the start of his next turn. The wind's power allows Haibram to swing and hurl the weapon with great force, causing the weapon to deal an additional 2d10 damage. If the weapon does not have the [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) trait, it also gains the thrown 10 feet trait during this attack.

All creatures within a 30-foot emanation must succeed at a DC 44 Fortitude saving throw or fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) and become [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) (stunned for 1 round on a critical failure).

**Oviposit** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** The ironclad annihilator beetle has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** The ironclad annihilator injects its ovipositor under the grabbed creature's skin. The creature takes 4d6+20 bludgeoning damage (DC 44 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). On a failed save, the beetle implants an egg, and the target creature takes 4d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) that is unusually tenacious. The DC to stop the bleeding using [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=54) is 40, and healing the creature to full Hit Points doesn't automatically stop the bleeding. If the target creature dies from this bleed damage, its body is destroyed and unrecoverable except with powerful magic such as [_miracle_](/Spells.aspx?ID=196) or [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377), and an immature ironclad annihilator beetle (with statistics as a [giant stag beetle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=50) bursts forth.

**Sunder Objects**   When an ironclad annihilator damages an item or structure, it deals an additional 2d10 damage to that item or structure.

**[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39)** <actions string="Three Actions" />  Huge or smaller, foot, DC 44

</column>
name: Ironclad Annihilator Beetle

**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 44 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 44 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 [Wereant Sentinel](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1812) **Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 50 **Perception** +32; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); insect empathy, [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 120 feet **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +37, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +33, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +27 **Str** +10 **Dex** +5 **Con** +5 **Int** +0 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +6 **Items** _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [khopesh](/Weapons.aspx?ID=94)_, _[_major sturdy shield_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=327)_ ( (Hardness 17, HP 136, BT 68)) --- **AC** 43 (45 with shield raised) **Fort** +35 **Ref** +29 **Will** +32 **HP** 400 **Weaknesses** [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) 20 **Gnawing Retort** **Trigger** A creature adjacent to the wereant critically misses the wereant with a melee Strike; **Effect** The wereant makes a mandibles Strike against the triggering creature
**Retributive Strike** **Trigger** An enemy damages the wereant sentinel's ally, and both are within 15 feet of the wereant sentinel; **Effect** The ally gains resistance 20 against the triggering damage. If the foe is within reach, the wereant sentinel makes a melee Strike against it. --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** mandibles +36, **Damage** 3d10+20 slashing plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=20) and curse of the wereant **Melee** _khopesh_ +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 3d8+20 slashing **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The wereant changes into their humanoid, hybrid, or animal shape. Each shape has a specific, persistent appearance. A true wereant's natural form is their hybrid shape. In humanoid shape, they use their original humanoid size, lose their jaws and claw Strikes, and gain a melee fist Strike that deals bludgeoning damage equal to the slashing damage dealt by their mandibles. In animal shape, they take the form of a Medium giant ant, gain a climb Speed of 20 feet, gain [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) on their mandibles Strike, and lose their weapon Strikes. **Curse of the Wereant** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) This curse affects only [humanoids](/Traits.aspx?ID=91); **Saving Throw** DC 34 Fortitude. On each full moon, the cursed creature must succeed at another Fortitude save or turn into a wereant until dawn. The creature is under the GM's control and goes on a rampage for half the night before falling [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) until dawn. **Hasty Reposition** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The wereant sentinel Steps, moving up to 15 feet. **Moon Frenzy** ([Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) When a full moon appears in the night sky, the wereant must enter hybrid form, can't Change Shape, becomes one size larger, increases their reach by 5 feet, and increases the damage of their mandibles Strike by 2. When the moon sets or the sun rises, the wereant returns to humanoid form and is [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) for 2d4 hours. **Sentinel's Focus** The wereant sentinel plants its feet in defiance of those who would move it. It gains a +2 circumstance bonus to defenses against being [Shoved](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) or [Tripped](/Actions.aspx?ID=40), and its melee Strikes deal an additional 2d8 mental damage. These effects end when it moves from its square using anything other than Hasty Reposition.
name: Wereant Sentinel

markdown:
While most nymphs bond with a natural feature, such as a tree, pool, or cave, horai are rare nymphs associated with a specific time—the summer or winter solstice—rather than a location. Horai erect simple astronomical calendars near their home, which they use to mark the moment when the sun reaches its highest or lowest point in the sky. An hora tied to the summer solstice grows more powerful as the sun reaches its height, while an hora tied to the winter solstice is sickly and weak. The opposite holds true for winter horai, who are weak in summer and strong in winter. Meanwhile, autumn and spring horai draw power near the autumnal and vernal equinoxes, becoming weaker around the opposite equinox.
Summer horai appear constantly bathed in sunlight, with skin and clothing of red, orange, and yellow, like the sunlight of high summer. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Summer Hora](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1813) **Source** [Pathfinder #177: Burning Tundra](/Sources.aspx?ID=116) pg. 80 **Perception** +14; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_speak with animals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=293), [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +13, [Astronomy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +15, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +13, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +15, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +14 **Str** +1 **Dex** +5 **Con** +2 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 **Summer Bound** During the winter months, a summer hora can't take reactions, is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and can't reduce their sickened condition below 1. --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +14 **Will** +17 **HP** 90 **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 5 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5 **Solstice Dependent** An hora is mystically bonded to a natural location with a simple astronomical calendar that marks the summer and winter solstice. If they aren't at that location at dawn when the sunlight touches the calendar, they become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), increasing the value by 1 for each missed day and reducing by 1 only when they witness dawn's light touch the calendar. Even on a cloudy day, there's enough sunlight to satisfy the hora's need. An hora can perform a 24-hour ritual to bond to a new location. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** solar blade +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d8+4 slashing plus 1d6 fire **Ranged** solar ray +17 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d12+2 fire **Primal Innate Spells** DC 24 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Know Direction](/Spells.aspx?ID=169), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171) - **1st** [Alarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=7), [Pass Without Trace](/Spells.aspx?ID=215) (at will) - **2nd** [Animal Messenger](/Spells.aspx?ID=11), [Faerie Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=107) - **3rd** [Animal Vision](/Spells.aspx?ID=12), [Curse of Lost Time](/Spells.aspx?ID=683) - **Constant (2nd)** [Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=293) - **Constant (4th)** [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **Summer's Salvation** **Trigger** The summer hora or an ally within 30 feet that the summer hora can see fails a Perception check or saving throw; **Effect** If it's the month of the summer solstice, the hora's or ally's check retroactively gains a +4 status bonus, which can change the outcome from a critical failure to a failure or a failure to a success. If it's the month before or after the summer solstice, the check gains a retroactive +2 status bonus instead. In the other months except during the three winter solstice months, the check gains a retroactive +1 status bonus instead. During the winter solstice months, this ability has no effect. The summer hora can't use Summer's Fortune again for 1d4 rounds.
Other Horai The statistics here are for summer horai, but you can represent the other three types of horai with the following alterations. Winter horai have resistance to [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) rather than to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) and Strikes that deal cold damage rather than fire damage. Similarly, autumn horai have resistance to [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118) energy and Strikes that deal negative damage; spring horai have no resistance and Strikes that deal positive damage (which only harms [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) and creatures with [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), as normal). Winter horai have the Winter Bound ability which impairs them during the summer months, and winter horai queens reverse the effects of their Tied to the Land ability (their land is healthy during the winter and withers during the summer). Similarly, autumn and spring horai are impaired during the opposing months, and their queens' Tied to the Land abilities change accordingly. Winter horai have Winter's Rescue rather than Summer's Salvation, which gives a +4 status bonus during the month of the winter solstice (Kuthona on Golarion), a +2 status bonus on the months before and after it (Neth and Abadius), a +1 bonus on most other months, and no benefit during the three summer months. Similarly, autumn and spring horai have reactions that grant a +4 status bonus during the month of their equinox, a +2 status bonus on the months just before and after, a +1 status bonus on other months, and no benefit during the opposing equinox and the months just before and after.
name: Summer Hora

markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="">[Tar Ooze](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1815)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

When creatures carrying magical items die in a tar pit, the magic mixes with the tar itself. Over centuries, this energy animates the tar into a relentless and horrifying ooze.

 Tar oozes begin their existence in tar pits, naturally occurring lakes of thick and sticky petroleum. Tar pits make effective traps for animals and humanoids alike, as they're often covered with thin layers of dirt or grass or reflect sunlight in a way that resembles a watering hole. Once a creature steps or falls into the sticky tar of the pit, escape is all but impossible. Slowly, the caught creature—and any other victims lured near by its plaintive cries—sinks into the tar, suffocates, and dies.

 Magic inherent in a captive creature or carried in powerful magic items carried by the dead remains inside the tar, where it gathers, mixes, and slowly energizes it until it begins to move and seek prey. A tar ooze seeps slowly over the land, catching anything it touches in a sticky embrace and devouring it.

 Living things consumed by the ooze slowly have their flesh digested, only for the bones and fur to be replaced with blobs of sticky tar. These unnatural zombies eventually crawl away, hungry for flesh. Without a master to give them instructions, these [tar zombies](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=303) often follow the ooze that created them, swarming anyone bold or foolish enough to get in their way.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Ooze](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 29

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Ooze_TarOoze.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 10">[Tar Ooze](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1815)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Huge" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=108" />
<trait label="Ooze" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=122" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #177: Burning Tundra](/Sources.aspx?ID=116) pg. 82

**Perception** +18; motion sense 60 feet, no vision

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +23, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** -3

**Con** +8

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** -5
</row>

**Motion Sense** A tar ooze can sense nearby motion through vibration and movement.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 18 

**Fort** +22 

**Ref** +10 

**Will** +15 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 255

</row>

**Immunities**
critical hits, [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)

**Weaknesses**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10

**Sticky** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The tar ooze is hit by a melee attack; **Effect** The ooze attempts to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) the creature that hit it. The ooze must still spend an action to extend the duration of the Grab on subsequent rounds, as normal.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 15 feet, climb 10 feet; viscous sludge

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
pseudopod +23 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d10+14 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27)

**[Engulf](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=14)** <actions string="Two Actions" />  DC 29, 6d6 bludgeoning, [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC 29, Rupture 17

**Viscous Sludge**   The tar ooze can have up to eight creatures grappled at the same time; doing so doesn't prevent it from making pseudopod Strikes. A tar ooze can climb on ceilings and other inverted surfaces, though such surfaces are difficult terrain.

**Zombification**  ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Ooze](/Traits.aspx?ID=122)) A creature slain while engulfed by a tar ooze undergoes a horrifying transformation and crawls from the ooze a week later as a [tar zombie](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=303).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Tar Ooze Treasure</title>

A tar ooze is animated by the mixed magical auras of creatures and items trapped within it. Unlike most oozes, a tar ooze isn't acidic; it is massive, however, and objects caught inside are subjected to great pressure and force as the tar ooze moves. Anything small and hard— such as coins, jewels, rings, and [_aeon stones_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407)—are especially likely to remain relatively undamaged inside a tar ooze. Other metal objects, including most weapons and armor, are also likely to survive, though some might need repair.
</aside>
name: Tar Ooze

Beneath the camps of Hillcross, Mammoth Lord followings store belongings both precious and mundane in unsecured vaults called weykos. As power changes hands among Mammoth Lords and followings go extinct or become forgotten, such weykos are often abandoned for so long that they become indistinguishable from trash heaps or middens. Furthermore, if the conditions are just so, such weykos can spring to life as a result of latent primal magic or ancestral memories locked within the forgotten weyko that refuse to be forgotten. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Weykoward](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1816) **Source** [Pathfinder #177: Burning Tundra](/Sources.aspx?ID=116) pg. 83 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +17 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** -4 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +19 **Will** +16 **HP** 145 **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374)) **Aura of Discarded Things** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) 30 feet. Items in the emanation can't regain Hit Points unless the effect that attempts to repair them [counteracts](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the aura, which has a counteract level of 5 and a counteract DC of 30.
**Debris Shield** **Trigger** The weykoward is targeted by an attack from an attacker it can see; **Effect** The weykoward intercepts the attack with junk, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to its AC against the triggering attack. If the attack misses and the attacker is within the weykoward's reach, it can [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) the attacker. It doesn't need a hand free to Shove. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +23 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d12+13 bludgeoning plus [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=25) **Wasting Gaze** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The weykoward glances at an item of 4 Bulk or less it can see within 30 feet. A creature holding or wearing the object must attempt a DC 26 Will save. An unattended item always critically fails this save.
**Critical Success** The targeted item is unaffected. All of the creature's equipment is immune to Wasting Gaze for 24 hours.
**Success** The item is unaffected.
**Failure** The item loses 10d6 Hit Points; this can make the item [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2), but it can't reduce the target's Hit Points beyond its Broken Threshold.
**Critical Failure** The target item loses 20d6 Hit Points. This can't reduce the item below 1 Hit Point.
Spontaneous Guardians Most weykowards across Golarion guard out of the way places whose people moved on long ago. These weykowards are primarily a danger to explorers or tomb robbers who intrude on the areas they defend. Weykowards that arise in societies where cultural practices of trash disposal remain unchanged for millennia, such as the Five Kings Mountains and the Realm of the Mammoth Lords, defend locals as fervently as they do their middens.
name: Weykoward

**Vomit Tar** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The tar zombie vomits a gout of sticky tar in a 15-foot cone that deals 5d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Creatures that fail the save are [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) and gain fire weakness 5. Both conditions end after 3 rounds or when the tar is scrubbed off (which requires 3 Interact actions). The tar zombie can't Vomit Tar again for 1d4 rounds.

The tar zombie can't Vomit Tar again for 1d4 rounds.

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 33 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 [Yana No-Trail](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1820) **Source** [Pathfinder #177: Burning Tundra](/Sources.aspx?ID=116) pg. 7 **Perception** +13; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Hallit](/Languages.aspx?ID=37), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Skald](/Languages.aspx?ID=42); [_speak with animals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=293) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +17, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +17, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +17, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +17 **Str** +1 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +3 **Wis** +0 **Cha** +4 **Items** [_standard potion of flying_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=189) --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +19 **Will** +11 **HP** 110 **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 3 **Cold Tolerant** Yana treats [environmental cold effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) as if they were one step less extreme.
**Evasion** When Yana rolls a success on a Reflex save, he gets a critical success instead.
**Nimble Dodge** **Trigger** Yana is targeted by a melee or ranged attack from an attacker he can see; **Effect** Yana gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 25 feet, climb 10 feet **Melee** claw +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)), **Damage** 2d4+3 slashing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 23 - **2nd** [Animal Messenger](/Spells.aspx?ID=11), [Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=293) (at will) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Kitsune](/Traits.aspx?ID=341), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Yana can transform into an Ulfen [human](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6), Tiny fox, or hybrid kitsune form. Using Change Shape counts as creating a disguise for the [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) use of [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5). He can remain in any of his forms indefinitely. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** Yana deals an additional 3d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.
name: Yana No-Trail

**Counter-Charge** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature Strides within reach of the warrior and critically fails to Strike the warrior with a melee weapon; **Effect** The rhino warrior knocks the creature to the ground, and the triggering creature falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) and takes 2d6+5 bludgeoning damage. The rhino warrior is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the end of their next turn.

</column>

---

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
pick +14 ([fatal d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=178)),
**Damage** 1d6+7 piercing

**Dig In** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The rhino warrior makes two pick Strikes against a creature within reach. The rhino warrior rolls only one attack roll for both Strikes, then compares the result against the creature's AC. The rhino warrior's multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after both Strikes.

</column>
name: Rhino Warrior

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Giant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=79" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #177: Burning Tundra](/Sources.aspx?ID=116) pg. 19

**Perception** +16; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +16

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +3

**Con** +5

**Int** +3

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Items**
[Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [greataxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=21)_, [sack with 10 firebombs](/Equipment.aspx?ID=45) (see Throw Bomb)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 26 

**Fort** +19 

**Ref** +16 

**Will** +13 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 100

</row>

**Immunities**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27)

**Weaknesses**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet; ice stride

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_greataxe_ +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)),
**Damage** 2d12+8 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
foot +18 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d8+9 bludgeoning

**Ignite Greataxe** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The Graylok artillerist combines gelatin and other substances over their greataxe to ignite it. For 1 minute, the greataxe gains the [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) trait, and creatures hit by it take 4 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

**Ice Stride**   A frost giant isn't impeded by difficult terrain caused by snow or ice, nor do they need to attempt [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks to keep from falling on slippery ice.

**Throw Bomb** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Alchemical](/Traits.aspx?ID=6), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) **Requirements** The Graylok artillerist has two free hands; **Effect** The Graylok artillerist Interacts with a firebomb and then throws it at a point within 100 feet. The bomb erupts in a 10-foot burst, and works as [moderate alchemist fire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=75), except it inflicts 4d6 fire damage with a DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. Creatures that fail or critically fail this save also take 4 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

**Wide Swing** <actions string="Single Action" />  The frost giant makes a single greataxe Strike and compares the attack roll result to the ACs of up to two foes within their reach. This counts as two attacks for the frost giant's multiple attack penalty.

</column>
name: Graylok Artillerist

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +16; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8)

**Skills**

**Items**
battering ram, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [battle axe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=16)_, [Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10), [large tower shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=4) (Hardness 5, HP 30, BT 15)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 28 (30 with shield raised, 32 behind cover)

**Fort** +21 

**Ref** +14 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 130

</row>

**Immunities**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27)

**Weaknesses**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10

**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32)** <actions string="Reaction" /> 

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet; ice stride

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_battleaxe_ +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)),
**Damage** 2d8+9 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
foot +20 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d8+7 bludgeoning

**Freeze Blade** <actions string="Single Action" />  The frost giant gatebreaker breathes on the blade of their battleaxe, coating it in a layer of magical ice. For 1 minute, the frost giant's battleaxe Strikes gain the [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) trait, and creatures struck by the battleaxe are [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35); at the end of each of their turns, the slowed creature can attempt a DC 23 Fortitude save to end the condition.

**Ice Stride**   A frost giant isn't impeded by difficult terrain caused by snow or ice, nor do they need to attempt [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks to keep from falling on slippery ice.

**Shielded Stride**   When the frost giant gatebreaker has their shield up, they can Stride to move half their Speed without triggering reactions that are triggered by their movement (such as [Attack of Opportunity](/Actions.aspx?ID=8)).

**Trampling Ram** <actions string="Three Actions" />  **Requirements** Another frost giant gatebreaker has Readied to Stride on the frost giant gatebreaker's order; **Effect** The two frost giants Stride, with a maximum distance equal to the slowest giant's Speed. Every creature whose space they move through takes 2d8+9 bludgeoning damage (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save); creatures who fail their save fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). At the end of their movement, the giants make a single Strike with a +20 modifier (reach 15 feet), inflicting 4d6+16 bludgeoning damage on a hit and knocking the target prone. If the giants hit, they can immediately attempt to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) the creature as a free action.

</column>
name: Graylok Gatebreaker

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Graylok Ambusher](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1824) **Source** [Pathfinder #177: Burning Tundra](/Sources.aspx?ID=116) pg. 21 **Perception** +19; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +16 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Items** [Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [hatchet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=27)_ --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +19 **Will** +13 **HP** 130 **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10 **Deny Advantage** A Graylok ambusher isn't [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39), or flanking creatures of 8th level or lower or creatures of 8th level or lower using sneak attack. However, such creatures can still help other creatures flank.
**Opportune Backstab** **Trigger** A creature within melee reach is hit by a melee attack from one of the Graylok ambusher's allies; **Effect** The Graylok ambusher attempts a Strike against the triggering creature. --- **Speed** 30 feet; ice stride **Melee** _hatchet_ +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 2d6+9 slashing **Melee** fist +18 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6+8 bludgeoning **Brutal Beating** When the Graylok ambusher scores a critical hit with a melee Strike, the target is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19). **Dread Striker** Any creature that has the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against the Graylok ambusher's attacks. **Ice Stride** A frost giant isn't impeded by difficult terrain caused by snow or ice, nor do they need to attempt [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks to keep from falling on slippery ice. **Light Step** When the Graylok ambusher Strides or Steps, they ignore difficult terrain. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The Graylok ambusher deals an additional 2d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.
name: Graylok Ambusher

([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145)

**Resistances**

**Weaknesses**

Axe Vulnerability An arboreal tar tree takes 10 additional damage from [axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1).

</column>

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
thorny branch +19 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d8+11 slashing plus [improved grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
rock +19 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 2d6+10 bludgeoning

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 25
- **2nd**
[Shape Wood](/Spells.aspx?ID=277) (at will)
- **3rd**
[Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=94), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=289), [Wall of Thorns](/Spells.aspx?ID=366)
- **5th**
[Black Tentacles](/Spells.aspx?ID=23), [Cloudkill](/Spells.aspx?ID=42), [Tree Stride](/Spells.aspx?ID=343) (only between dead trees)

**Spawn Tar Tree**  ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) A living [arboreal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=8) or animated tree slain by a tar tree's thorny branch Strike rises as a tar tree spawn after 1d4 rounds. This tar tree spawn is under the command of the tar tree that killed it. It doesn't have primal innate spells or the spawn tar tree ability and becomes [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for as long as it's a tar tree spawn. If the creator of the tar tree spawn dies, the tar tree spawn becomes a full-fledged, autonomous tar tree; it regains its free will, gains primal innate spells and the spawn tar tree ability, and is no longer clumsy.

**[Throw Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=38)** <actions string="Single Action" />  

</column>
name: Arboreal Tar Tree

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 37

<title level="2" right="Creature 10">[Calcifda](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1826)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="LE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=69" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #177: Burning Tundra](/Sources.aspx?ID=116) pg. 40

**Perception** +21; gemsight

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Cyclops](/Languages.aspx?ID=30), [Hallit](/Languages.aspx?ID=37), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10), [Terran](/Languages.aspx?ID=22)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19, [Gem Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +21, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +19, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +21

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +4

**Con** +5

**Int** +7

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Gemsight** As long as Calcifda holds a gemstone, she can see through the gem with [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) and the effects of [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344). When she uses her _pearlescent pyramid aeon stone_ for this ability, she also knows the [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) and [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) value of every creature she sees. Calcifda is blind when she isn't holding a gem in her hand.

**Items**
[_pearlescent pyramid aeon stone_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407), opal worth 25 gp

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 31 

**Fort** +19 

**Ref** +17 

**Will** +23 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 140

</row>

**Immunities**
[paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)

**Resistances**
physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374))

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5

Light Sickness Calcifda is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) in bright light.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, burrow 25 feet; earth glide

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 3d6+11 slashing plus coal curse

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 29
- **Cantrips (5th)**
[Know Direction](/Spells.aspx?ID=169), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246)
- **2nd**
[Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=15), [Undetectable Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=348)
- **3rd**
[Clairaudience](/Spells.aspx?ID=39), [Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=94)
- **4th**
[Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=40), [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=247), [Shape Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=276)
- **5th**
[Incendiary Fog](/Spells.aspx?ID=1102), [Summon Giant](/Spells.aspx?ID=324) (cyclops), [Wall of Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=365)

**Coal Curse**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Wounds dealt by Calcifda's claws stiffen flesh and turn the blood that runs from them into tar. Each time a creature is damaged by Calcifda's claw Strike, it must succeed at a DC 29 Fortitude save or become permanently [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) (slowed 2 on a critical failure) as its flesh stiffens like stone. If a creature is reduced to 0 Hit Points from Calcifda's claw Strike and fails the saving throw against coal curse, it's [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30) and turned to coal. A creature that spends 8 hours in direct sunlight can attempt a new saving throw to remove the effects of coal curse, even if it has been petrified.

**Earth Glide**   Calcifda can Burrow through any earthen matter, including rock. When she does so, she moves at her full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of her passing.

**Gem Gaze** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) Calcifda holds aloft a gem and gazes into the mind of a creature within 30 feet, infusing the creature's thoughts with visions of their body slowly turning to coal. The creature must succeed at a DC 29 Will save or become [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (frightened 2 on a critical failure).

**Lashing Stone** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** Calcifda makes a claw Strike that originates from any stony or tarry surface within 60 feet. A creature that fails its Fortitude save against coal curse from this Strike increases its [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) value by 1 (slowed 2 on a failed save, slowed 3 on a critical failure).

**Steady Spellcasting**   If a reaction would disrupt Calcifda's spellcasting action, she attempts a DC 15 flat check. On a success, the action isn't disrupted.

</column>
name: Calcifda
